name: backend-pipeline
stdout:
Phase 1을 완료했습니다. 표준 라이브러리만으로 동작하는 백엔드 파이프라인을 [backend/app/models.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/backend/app/models.py:35), [backend/app/parser.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/backend/app/parser.py:14), [backend/app/analyzer.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/backend/app/analyzer.py:37), [backend/app/server.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/backend/app/server.py:24)에 구현했고, `GET /health`, `GET /api/sample`, `POST /api/analyze`와 정적 파일 서빙까지 포함했습니다. 샘플 공고, 보수적 자격 판정, 경고 추출, 체크리스트/액션 링크 생성도 같이 들어갔습니다. 검증용 `unittest`는 [tests/backend/test_analysis.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/tests/backend/test_analysis.py:11), [tests/backend/test_server.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/tests/backend/test_server.py:13)에 추가했습니다.

검증은 `python -m compileall backend`, `python -m unittest discover -s tests/backend -p "test_*.py"`로 수행했고 둘 다 통과했습니다. `tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json`의 phase 1 상태도 [completed로 갱신](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json:16)했고 완료 시각은 `2026-05-19T10:07:53+0900`입니다. 참고로 `tasks/0-docmate-mvp/docs-diff.md`는 현재 작업 폴더가 git 저장소가 아니라 자동 생성되지 않은 상태입니다.

-TotalCount 250" in C:\Users\DO\Desktop\docmate succeeded in 740ms: --- name: plan-and-build description: >- Codex 기반 자율 구현 하네스. 사용자의 요구를 받아 초기기획 → clarify(9단계) → context-gather → plan(task/phase 분해) → generate(codex 직렬 실행) → evaluate 파이프라인을 단계별 sub-agent로 실행한다. 각 단계는 전용 prompt 파일을 따르며 결과를 artifact 파일로 다음 단계에 전달한다. 사용자가 "하네스 시작", "plan-and-build", "task 만들어줘", "phase 실행" 등을 말할 때 사용한다. disable-model-invocation: true --- # plan-and-build Codex CLI 기반 자율 구현 하네스의 메인 orchestrator. 모든 sub-agent prompt와 artifact는 파일 시스템을 통해 전달된다. ## 핵심 원칙 1. **단계별 sub-agent**: 각 stage는 `agents/0N-*.md`의 prompt를 따르는 별도 sub-agent로 동작한다. orchestrator는 stage 진입 시 해당 파일을 읽고 그 페르소나/지시를 채택한다. 2. **artifact 기반 전달**: 모든 stage 출력은 `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/0N-*.md`에 저장한다. 다음 stage는 이전 artifact를 반드시 읽고 시작한다. 3. **사용자 프롬프트 자동 기록**: clarify 9단계 동안 사용자가 입력한 모든 프롬프트 원문을 `tasks/{id}-{name}/prompts/clarify/0N-*.md`에 즉시 저장한다. 4. **로컬 CLI 우선**: 모든 구현은 로컬 CLI만으로 가능해야 한다. 사용자 개입이 불가피한 지점은 `docs/user-intervention.md`에 명시한다. 5. **brand-new repo 친화**: context-gather와 evaluate는 코드베이스 상황에 따라 스킵 가능하다. ## 디렉토리 규약 ``` .codex/skills/plan-and-build/ SKILL.md ← 본 파일 agents/ 01-initial-plan.md ← 초기기획 sub-agent 02-clarify.md ← clarify 9단계 sub-agent 03-context-gather.md ← context gather sub-agent 04-plan.md ← task/phase 생성 sub-agent 05-evaluate.md ← MVP 후 평가 sub-agent prompts/ task-created.md ← task/phase 파일 생성 규격 (plan stage가 따름) scripts/ run_phases.py ← codex 직렬 실행 runner gen_docs_diff.py ← phase 0 후 docs-diff.md 생성 _utils.py ← 공용 유틸 tasks/ index.json ← 전체 task 목록 {id}-{name}/ index.json ← 이 task의 phase 목록 artifacts/ 01-initial-plan.md 02-clarify.md 03-context.md ← (있을 경우) 05-evaluate.md ← (있을 경우) prompts/ clarify/ 01-feasibility.md ← clarify 9단계 사용자 프롬프트 원문 02-tech-stack.md ... 09-final-doc.md phase0.md phase1.md ... phaseN.md phase{N}-output.json ← runner가 저장하는 codex 출력 docs-diff.md ← phase 0 후 자동 생성 ``` ## 파이프라인 (5 + 1 stages) 진입 시 사용자 입력 한 줄 요구를 `$INITIAL_BRIEF`로 두고 아래 순서를 따른다. 각 stage 종료 시 artifact가 디스크에 존재하는지 검증한 뒤 다음 stage로 넘어간다. ### Stage 1. 초기기획 (initial-plan) `agents/01-initial-plan.md`를 읽고 그 지시대로 진행한다. 사용자와 대화하며 4가지를 확정한다: 요구사항 / 구현 디테일 / 제약 조건 / 완료 기준. 산출물: `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/01-initial-plan.md` 이 stage가 끝나면 task ID와 name이 결정되어야 한다 (kebab-case slug). ### Stage 2. clarify `agents/02-clarify.md`를 읽고 9개 sub-step을 순서대로 진행한다: 1. 구현가능성 검증 (feasibility) 2. 기술스택 선정 (tech-stack) 3. 사용흐름 점검 (user-flow) 4. 화면 설계 (ui-design) 5. API 설계 (api-design) 6. 데이터 설계 (data-design) 7. 코드아키텍처 설계 (architecture) 8. 기술적 결정사항 점검 (tech-decisions) 9. 최종문서 생성에 대한 논의점 (final-doc) **자동 기록 의무**: 각 sub-step 시작 시 사용자에게 질문하고, 사용자의 응답을 받자마자 그 원문을 `tasks/{id}-{name}/prompts/clarify/{NN}-{slug}.md`에 즉시 저장한다(요약/수정 금지). 산출물: `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/02-clarify.md` (9단계 합의 결과 요약) + `tasks/{id}-{name}/prompts/clarify/01~09-*.md` (사용자 프롬프트 원문) ### Stage 3. context-gather (옵션) 빈 코드베이스이면 **스킵**. 코드베이스 판정: ``` git ls-files | wc -l → 0 또는 README/설정 파일만 있으면 빈 코드베이스 ``` 스킵 시 `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/03-context.md`에 한 줄만 적는다: `Skipped: empty codebase.` 스킵 안 할 시 `agents/03-context-gather.md` 지시에 따라 explore 한 후 artifact 생성. ### Stage 4. plan `agents/04-plan.md`를 읽고, 추가로 `prompts/task-created.md`의 규격을 정확히 숙지한다. 이전 artifact 3개를 모두 input으로 받아 task/phase 파일을 생성한다. 산출물: - `tasks/index.json` 업데이트 - `tasks/{id}-{name}/index.json` - `tasks/{id}-{name}/phase0.md` (문서 업데이트 phase) - `tasks/{id}-{name}/phase1.md ~ phaseN.md` (구현 phase) ### Stage 5. generate `scripts/run_phases.py {task-dir}`를 실행한다. orchestrator가 직접 코드를 작성하지 않는다. runner는 각 phase를 독립 codex 세션으로 직렬 실행한다. ```bash python scripts/run_phases.py {id}-{name} ``` 실패 시 runner가 종료한다. 사용자에게 실패한 phase의 `index.json` status를 `pending`으로 되돌리고 재실행하라고 안내한다. ### Stage 6. evaluate (옵션) 간단한 MVP면 **스킵**. 스킵 판정 기준은 `agents/01-initial-plan.md`에서 사용자가 명시한 "완료 기준"을 본다 — 단순 동작 확인 수준이면 스킵. 스킵 안 할 시 `agents/05-evaluate.md`를 따른다. 산출물: `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/05-evaluate.md` ## Sub-agent 호출 방식 orchestrator(이 SKILL.md를 따르는 IDE 세션)는 각 stage 진입 시: 1. 해당 `agents/0N-*.md` 파일을 Read 도구로 읽는다. 2. 그 파일이 정의하는 페르소나/입력/출력 스펙을 채택한다. 3. 입력으로 명시된 이전 artifact들을 모두 Read 한다. 4. stage 작업을 수행한다 (사용자 인터랙션 또는 분석). 5. 명시된 artifact를 Write 한다. 6. artifact 존재 검증 후 다음 stage로. **plan stage**의 task/phase 파일 생성은 가능하면 별도 codex 서브세션으로 위임할 수 있지만, 필수는 아니다. orchestrator가 직접 작성해도 무방하다. **generate stage**는 항상 `scripts/run_phases.py` subprocess로만 실행한다 (orchestrator가 구현 코드를 절대 직접 작성하지 않는다). ## 시작 트리거 사용자 입력 예시: - "하네스 시작: <요구사항>" - "plan-and-build: <요구사항>" - "이거 만들어줘: <요구사항>" 이때 orchestrator는 즉시 Stage 1 (`agents/01-initial-plan.md`)로 진입한다. ## 재진입 / 부분 실행 사용자가 특정 stage만 다시 하길 원하면: - "clarify 다시" → Stage 2부터 재실행, 기존 artifact는 백업 후 덮어씀 - "phase 재실행" → `python scripts/run_phases.py {id}-{name}` 만 호출 - "task 만들기만 해줘" → Stage 4까지만 실행, generate는 사용자가 직접 ## 중요 규칙 - artifact 파일을 절대 건너뛰지 말 것. 모든 stage는 명시된 artifact를 생성해야 한다. - clarify 사용자 프롬프트는 받자마자 즉시 저장 (대화 종료 후 모아서 저장 금지). - task ID는 `tasks/index.json`의 마지막 ID + 1. 처음이면 0. - task name은 kebab-case slug, 1~2단어. - 모든 timestamp는 ISO 8601 + KST (`+0900`). 영역과 샘플 공고 사용 버튼. - 프로필 입력 영역: 나이, 학년, 거주지, 직업, 소득분위, 재학 여부. - 분석 실행 버튼과 loading/error/empty/result 상태. - 자격 결과 배너: 신청 가능, 추가 확인 필요, 신청 불가. - 핵심 정보 카드: 신청 기간, 지원 대상 조건, 혜택, 제출 서류, 신청 방법, 신청 URL. - 경고 카드: 수정 불가, 중복 지원 불가, 자동 탈락, 서류 미비 등. - 체크리스트: 실행 가능한 할 일, 관련 action URL, 완료 체크 상태. - 즉시 실행 영역: 신청 페이지 이동, 캘린더 등록 링크. 디자인 규칙: - 업무형 SaaS 도구처럼 차분하고 스캔하기 쉬운 화면을 만든다. - 카드 안에 카드를 중첩하지 않는다. - 첫 화면은 실제 기능 화면이어야 하며 마케팅 hero를 만들지 않는다. - 모바일과 데스크톱에서 텍스트가 버튼이나 패널 밖으로 넘치지 않게 한다. - 의미 없는 장식용 gradient/orb/bokeh를 넣지 않는다. API 연결: - `GET /api/sample`로 샘플 데이터를 불러온다. - `POST /api/analyze`로 파일과 프로필을 보내고 결과를 렌더링한다. - backend가 없을 때도 샘플 결과를 보여주는 fallback을 둔다. ## Acceptance Criteria ```bash node --check frontend/src/app.js python -m compileall backend python -m unittest discover -s tests/backend -p "test_*.py" ``` ## AC 검증 방법 위 AC 커맨드를 실행하라. 모두 통과하면 `tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json`의 phase 2 status를 `"completed"`로 변경하라. 수정 3회 이상 시도해도 실패하면 status를 `"error"`로 변경하고, 에러 내용을 index.json의 해당 phase에 `"error_message"` 필드로 기록하라. ## 주의사항 - npm install이 필요 없는 no-build 프론트엔드로 완성한다. PDF 파싱 라이브러리가 없으면 명확한 fallback 메시지와 sample provider 흐름을 유지한다. - `analyzer.analyze_document(text: str, profile: Profile) -> AnalysisResult`: 구조화 정보, 자격 결과, 경고, 체크리스트, action links를 반환한다. - `eligibility.evaluate_profile(extraction, profile)`: 신청 가능, 추가 확인 필요, 신청 불가 중 하나를 보수적으로 판정한다. - `checklist.build_checklist(extraction)`: 서류/절차를 행동 단위 체크리스트로 변환한다. - `server.py`: stdlib `http.server` 기반으로 `GET /health`, `GET /api/sample`, `POST /api/analyze`를 제공한다. 동시에 `frontend/` 정적 파일을 서빙할 수 있게 설계한다. 도메인 규칙: - 나이, 지역, 재학 여부처럼 단순한 필수 조건은 rule-based로 판정한다. - 프로필 정보가 비어 있거나 조건 해석이 불확실하면 `"needs_review"`로 판정한다. - 중복 지원 불가, 수정 불가, 자동 탈락, 서류 미비, 국적 제한 키워드는 경고로 추출한다. - API 키가 없어도 샘플 공고 분석 결과가 일관되게 나온다. ## Acceptance Criteria ```bash python -m compileall backend python -m unittest discover -s tests/backend -p "test_*.py" ``` ## AC 검증 방법 위 AC 커맨드를 실행하라. 모두 통과하면 `tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json`의 phase 1 status를 `"completed"`로 변경하라. 수정 3회 이상 시도해도 실패하면 status를 `"error"`로 변경하고, 에러 내용을 index.json의 해당 phase에 `"error_message"` 필드로 기록하라. ## 주의사항 - FastAPI가 설치되어 있지 않아도 동작해야 한다. C:\Users\DO\Desktop\docmate\docs\user-intervention.md succeeded in 1218ms: # DocMate MVP Spec ## Product Goal DocMate turns scholarship and youth-policy announcement documents into action-ready guidance. The MVP must help a user move from "I found a document" to "I know whether I can apply and what to do next" without requiring an API key or external service setup. Core outcomes: - Extract the key application details from an uploaded document. - Compare those details against a user profile and return a conservative 3-state eligibility result. - Surface warnings that commonly cause failed applications. - Convert requirements into a concrete checklist with direct action links. - Provide immediate next actions such as opening the application page and adding a deadline to a calendar flow. ## MVP Scope ### Supported Inputs - Text-based PDF documents, primarily 1 to 5 pages. - Plain text uploads or decodable file content for local testing and fallback flows. - Scholarship and youth-policy announcements for university students and young adults. ### Supported User Profile Fields - Age - Grade - Region - Occupation - Income decile - Enrollment status ### Supported Outputs - Structured summary of the announcement: - Title - Application period - Eligibility conditions - Benefits - Required documents - Application method - Application URL - Eligibility result: - `eligible` - `needs_review` - `ineligible` - Warning items for risky conditions such as: - Duplicate application restrictions - No-edit policies - Automatic rejection conditions - Missing-document rejection - Nationality restrictions - Actionable checklist items with optional related URLs. - Immediate actions for opening the application page and registering the deadline. ## Primary User Flow 1. The user uploads a document or chooses a built-in sample announcement. 2. The user enters profile information. 3. The system extracts document text. 4. The system analyzes the text into a structured extraction. 5. The system evaluates eligibility against the profile. 6. The system highlights warnings and generates a checklist. 7. The user follows the provided action links. ## Functional Requirements ### Document Analysis - The system must accept uploaded file content without permanently storing the original file on disk. - The system must extract text from decodable inputs directly. - The system must handle PDFs safely even when a PDF parsing library is unavailable. - The system must preserve a deterministic sample flow so the MVP demo works without external dependencies. ### Eligibility Evaluation - The system must use rule-based checks for simple conditions such as age, region, and enrollment. - The system must return `needs_review` when profile data is missing or the condition cannot be interpreted confidently. - The system must avoid optimistic guesses for complex nested conditions. ### Warning Detection - The system must detect and surface critical risk language when present in the source text. - The warning list must prioritize issues that can cause submission failure or disqualification. ### Checklist Generation - The system must convert required documents and application steps into action-oriented checklist items. - Checklist items should use imperative phrasing so the user can act immediately. ### Demo Reliability - The MVP must return a consistent sample analysis result without requiring: - API keys - OAuth setup - database provisioning - external network access ## Non-Goals The MVP explicitly excludes: - Login, subscription, billing, or account management - Persistent database storage and history - Real Google OAuth write access - Automatic document issuance from government or school portals - Image-heavy PDFs and OCR-first pipelines - Legal or contract documents with dense nested exceptions - Full automation for complex eligibility rules ## Implementation Constraints - The local MVP must be dependency-light and runnable in restricted environments. - The backend must work with the Python standard library only for the core demo path. - The frontend must render without npm install or external CDN assets. - Real LLM providers and richer frameworks remain extension points, not MVP requirements. ## Definition Of Done For The MVP - A user can complete the sample analysis flow end to end on a local machine without network access. - Structured extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and action links are all visible in the product flow. - The implementation remains aligned with the future path toward FastAPI, React, and real LLM providers, but does not depend on them now. succeeded in 1235ms: # DocMate MVP ADR ## ADR-001: Ship A Dependency-Light MVP First ### Decision Use the Python standard library and a no-build frontend as the baseline implementation path. ### Why - The execution environment may block package installation. - The project still needs a full demo and test flow without external setup. - Local reliability matters more than framework fidelity in the first milestone. ### Consequences - The current MVP uses `http.server` instead of FastAPI. - The frontend uses plain JavaScript instead of React. - The architecture must preserve upgrade seams for the originally planned stack. ## ADR-002: Keep A Deterministic Sample Provider ### Decision Include built-in sample data and deterministic analysis behavior that work without API keys. ### Why - The product must remain demoable in restricted environments. - Tests should not depend on network access or model variance. - Early product validation is about workflow usefulness, not model-provider sophistication. ### Consequences - Sample outputs must be stable across runs. - Real LLM integrations remain optional extension points. ## ADR-003: Prefer Conservative Eligibility Outcomes ### Decision Return `needs_review` whenever conditions are incomplete, ambiguous, or too complex for safe rule evaluation. ### Why - A false "eligible" result is more harmful than asking the user to verify a condition. - Scholarship and policy announcements often contain exceptions and edge cases. - The MVP only guarantees high confidence on simple rule patterns. ### Consequences - The product will intentionally avoid over-automation for complex documents. - Missing profile fields are treated as unresolved, not ignored. ## ADR-004: Make The First Screen The Working Tool ### Decision The initial UI must open directly into the analysis workspace rather than a marketing landing page. ### Why - The product value is demonstrated through action, not explanation. - The user flow starts with upload, profile input, and result inspection. - The MVP should minimize clicks between arrival and analysis. ### Consequences - The frontend prioritizes form and result density over promotional content. - Empty, loading, error, and result states are product-critical. ## ADR-005: Exclude Persistence And OAuth From The MVP ### Decision Do not implement database storage, login, or real calendar write access in the first milestone. ### Why - These features add setup and operational complexity without validating the core insight. - The immediate product question is whether action-oriented document analysis is useful. ### Consequences - Sessions are ephemeral. - Calendar support is limited to links or generated artifacts rather than direct account integration. - User intervention for keys and OAuth remains documented separately. succeeded in 1239ms: # DocMate MVP User Intervention ## Current MVP Requirement No user-managed API key, OAuth configuration, or external service account is required for the local MVP demo and test flow. The intended baseline experience is: 1. Start the local server. 2. Open the app. 3. Upload a supported file or use the sample announcement. 4. Review the generated analysis. ## Manual Actions The User May Still Perform - Choose a sample announcement instead of uploading a document when testing fallback behavior. - Verify final application details against the original announcement before submitting to the real institution. - Open external application or document-issuance links provided by the result screen. ## Not Required In This Phase The following are intentionally not required for MVP completion: - Claude or OpenAI API key setup - Google Calendar OAuth - Database provisioning - Cloud deployment credentials ## Future Optional Setup These setup steps may be introduced after the dependency-light MVP is validated: - `DOCMATE_LLM_PROVIDER` and provider-specific API key variables for real document analysis - Google OAuth client configuration for direct calendar event creation - Institution-specific link maps for school portals or government issuance sites If these future integrations are added, their setup instructions should stay in this document rather than being mixed into the core product spec. succeeded in 1274ms: # DocMate MVP Architecture ## Architecture Summary The implemented MVP uses a dependency-light local architecture: - Python backend with standard-library HTTP serving - Static no-build frontend - Deterministic analysis pipeline for demo and tests - Clear extension points for future FastAPI, React, and external LLM providers This is intentionally narrower than the long-term proposal. The goal of the current architecture is local reliability first. ## System Components ### Frontend - `frontend/index.html` - Single-screen work surface - Loads local JavaScript and CSS only - `frontend/src/app.js` - Handles upload, profile entry, sample loading, analysis requests, and result rendering - `frontend/src/styles.css` - Presents an operator-style tool UI optimized for scanning and action-taking Responsibilities: - Collect document and profile input - Call backend APIs - Render eligibility, extraction, warnings, checklist, and action links - Fall back to sample data when backend access is unavailable ### Backend - `backend/app/models.py` - Core data structures for profile, extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and analysis response - `backend/app/parser.py` - Converts uploaded content into text - Handles safe PDF fallback behavior - `backend/app/analyzer.py` - Produces structured extraction and warning candidates - Coordinates sample provider behavior - `backend/app/eligibility.py` - Applies conservative rule-based eligibility evaluation - `backend/app/checklist.py` - Converts extracted requirements into actionable checklist items - `backend/app/sample_data.py` - Supplies deterministic sample announcement text and expected profile/result data - `backend/app/server.py` - Exposes HTTP endpoints and static file serving - `backend/run.py` - Starts the local server ### Tests - `tests/backend/test_analysis.py` - Verifies pipeline behavior and rule outcomes - `tests/backend/test_server.py` - Verifies HTTP routes and payload handling ## Runtime Flow 1. The user opens the frontend from the same server that exposes the API. 2. The frontend requests `GET /api/sample` for the sample workflow or submits `POST /api/analyze`. 3. The backend parses the uploaded content into text. 4. The analyzer extracts structured fields and warning candidates. 5. The eligibility module evaluates the extraction against the profile. 6. The checklist module turns extracted requirements into action items. 7. The backend returns a single structured analysis response to the frontend. Supporting route: - `GET /health` returns a lightweight status check. ## API Shape ### `GET /health` Response: ```json { "status": "ok" } ``` ### `GET /api/sample` Response includes: - Sample source text - Sample profile - Sample analysis result or enough data to build one deterministically ### `POST /api/analyze` Request: - Multipart form data or another dependency-light format carrying: - uploaded file content or raw text - profile fields Response: ```json { "extraction": {}, "eligibility": {}, "warnings": [], "checklist": [], "actions": [] } ``` Errors must use a simple JSON shape such as: ```json { "detail": "..." } ``` ## Data Model Overview ### Profile - `age` - `grade` - `region` - `occupation` - `income_decile` - `enrolled` ### DocumentExtraction - `title` - `application_period` - `eligibility_conditions` - `benefits` - `required_documents` - `application_method` - `application_url` ### EligibilityResult - `status` - `reasons` - `missing_information` ### WarningItem - `severity` - `title` - `evidence` ### ChecklistItem - `title` - `description` - `action_url` - `done` ### AnalysisResult - `extraction` - `eligibility` - `warnings` - `checklist` - `actions` ## Design Constraints - No persistent database in the MVP - No external network requirement for tests or demo - No dependency on FastAPI, React, or third-party PDF parsing libraries for the base flow - No file persistence for user uploads ## Extension Path The architecture keeps explicit upgrade points: - Replace the standard-library server with FastAPI when dependency freedom is no longer required. - Replace the no-build frontend with React + TypeScript when a richer component model is needed. - Add provider adapters for Claude or OpenAI after API key setup is available. - Add Google Calendar OAuth once the MVP proves user value. These are planned extensions, not current requirements. 2026-05-19T01:01:26.628586Z ERROR codex_core::tools::router: error=Exit code: 1 Wall time: 1.3 seconds Output: exited 1 in 1319ms: succeeded in 1364ms: # Clarify Summary: docmate-mvp ## 1. 구현가능성 - MVP는 구현 가능하다. PDF 텍스트 추출, 구조화 분석, 자격 판정, 체크리스트 생성, 경고 탐지는 독립 모듈로 나눌 수 있다. - 실제 LLM API 키와 Google Calendar OAuth는 사용자 환경 의존성이므로, 첫 구현에서는 deterministic sample provider와 캘린더 등록 링크 또는 ICS 생성으로 대체한다. - 복잡한 중첩 조건, 표/이미지 중심 PDF, 법률 문서는 MVP 범위 밖으로 둔다. ## 2. 기술스택 | 항목 | 선택 | |------|------| | 프론트엔드 | React + TypeScript + Vite | | 백엔드 | FastAPI + Python 3.11 | | 문서 파싱 | PyMuPDF 우선, 미설치/실패 시 텍스트 샘플 fallback | | AI 분석 | provider 인터페이스 + sample provider, 추후 Claude/OpenAI provider 확장 | | 자격 판정 | rule-based 비교 + 불확실 조건 보수 처리 | | 데이터 저장 | MVP에서는 DB 없음, 요청/응답 JSON 중심 | | 테스트 | pytest, frontend smoke는 build/test script | ## 3. 사용흐름 1. 사용자는 PDF를 업로드하거나 샘플 공고를 선택한다. 2. 사용자는 나이, 학년, 거주지, 직업, 소득분위, 재학 여부 같은 프로필을 입력한다. 3. 시스템은 문서 텍스트를 추출하고 구조화 결과, 자격 상태, 경고, 체크리스트, 행동 링크를 생성한다. 4. 사용자는 결과 카드를 확인하고 신청 페이지 또는 서류 발급/캘린더 등록 액션으로 이동한다. ## 4. 화면 설계 - 단일 작업형 화면을 첫 화면으로 둔다. - 좌측 또는 상단에는 PDF 업로드/샘플 선택과 프로필 입력 폼을 배치한다. - 결과 영역에는 자격 상태 배너, 핵심 정보 카드, 경고 카드, 제출 서류/체크리스트, 신청/캘린더 액션을 표시한다. - 모바일에서는 입력 영역과 결과 영역을 세로 흐름으로 배치한다. ## 5. API 설계 | METHOD | PATH | 입력 | 출력 | |--------|------|------|------| | GET | `/health` | 없음 | `{ "status": "ok" }` | | GET | `/api/sample` | 없음 | 샘플 분석 요청/결과에 쓸 문서 텍스트와 프로필 | | POST | `/api/analyze` | multipart PDF 또는 raw text, profile JSON | 구조화 정보, 자격 결과, 경고, 체크리스트, 액션 링크 | 오류 응답은 `{ "detail": "..." }` 형태를 따른다. MVP 인증은 없다. ## 6. 데이터 설계 - `Profile`: age, grade, region, occupation, incomeDecile, enrolled - `DocumentExtraction`: title, applicationPeriod, eligibilityConditions, benefits, requiredDocuments, applicationMethod, applicationUrl - `EligibilityResult`: status, reasons, missingInformation - `WarningItem`: severity, title, evidence - `ChecklistItem`: title, description, actionUrl, done - `AnalysisResult`: extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, actions DB와 migration은 두지 않는다. 브라우저 상태와 API 응답으로 MVP를 완성한다. ## 7. 코드 아키텍처 - `backend/app/main.py`: FastAPI app과 route 등록 - `backend/app/models.py`: Pydantic request/response schema - `backend/app/parser.py`: PDF/text 추출 - `backend/app/analyzer.py`: provider 인터페이스와 sample analysis - `backend/app/eligibility.py`: profile 조건 비교 - `backend/app/checklist.py`: 행동 체크리스트와 링크 생성 - `frontend/src/App.tsx`: 화면 composition - `frontend/src/api.ts`: backend API client - `frontend/src/components/`: upload, profile, result components - `tests/`: backend service/API 테스트 ## 8. 기술 결정사항 (ADR 요약) - LLM provider를 분리한다: API 키가 없는 환경에서도 데모와 테스트가 가능해야 한다. - DB를 제외한다: MVP 검증 목표는 분석 경험이며, 저장/히스토리는 프리미엄 또는 고도화 단계다. - 복합 조건은 보수적으로 처리한다: 잘못된 신청 가능 판정보다 추가 확인 필요가 사용자 신뢰에 유리하다. - 첫 화면을 실제 도구로 만든다: DocMate의 차별점은 설명이 아니라 행동 연결이다. ## 9. 구현 계획 논의점 - Phase 0에서 `docs/spec.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/adr.md`를 만든다. - Phase 1에서 백엔드 schema, parser, analyzer, eligibility, checklist, tests를 만든다. - Phase 2에서 프론트엔드 작업형 UI와 API 연결을 만든다. - Phase 3에서 샘플 데이터, 통합 실행 스크립트, 빌드/테스트 검증을 정리한다. - 위험 영역은 PDF 파서 의존성, 실제 LLM API, 캘린더 OAuth다. 모두 fallback prompts/clarify/ ← clarify 9단계 사용자 프롬프트 원문 phase{N}.md ← codex가 실행할 phase 파일 phase{N}-output.json docs-diff.md ← 자동 생성 docs/ ← phase 0가 갱신/생성하는 프로젝트 문서 ``` ## 사용 orchestrator(IDE의 코딩 에이전트)에게 한 줄 요구를 plan-and-build\ndescription: >-\n Codex 기반 자율 구현 하네스. 사용자의 요구를 받아 초기기획 → clarify(9단계) →\n context-gather → plan(task/phase 분해) → generate(codex 직렬 실행) → evaluate\n 파이프라인을 단계별 sub-agent로 실행한다. 각 단계는 전용 prompt 파일을 따르며\n 결과를 artifact 파일로 다음 단계에 전달한다. 사용자가 \"하네스 시작\",\n \"plan-and-build\", \"task 만들어줘\", \"phase 실행\" 등을 말할 때 사용한다.\ndisable-model-invocation: true\n---\n\n# plan-and-build\n\nCodex CLI 기반 자율 구현 하네스의 메인 orchestrator. 모든 sub-agent prompt와 artifact는\n파일 시스템을 통해 전달된다.\n\n## 핵심 원칙\n\n1. **단계별 sub-agent**: 각 stage는 `agents/0N-*.md`의 prompt를 따르는 별도 sub-agent로 동작한다.\n orchestrator는 stage 진입 시 해당 파일을 읽고 그 페르소나/지시를 채택한다.\n2. **artifact 기반 전달**: 모든 stage 출력은 `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/0N-*.md`에 저장한다.\n 다음 stage는 이전 artifact를 반드시 읽고 시작한다.\n3. **사용자 프롬프트 자동 기록**: clarify 9단계 동안 사용자가 입력한 모든 프롬프트 원문을\n `tasks/{id}-{name}/prompts/clarify/0N-*.md`에 즉시 저장한다.\n4. **로컬 CLI 우선**: 모든 구현은 로컬 CLI만으로 가능해야 한다. 사용자 개입이 불가피한 지점은\n `docs/user-intervention.md`에 명시한다.\n5. **brand-new repo 친화**: context-gather와 evaluate는 코드베이스 상황에 따라 스킵 가능하다.\n\n## 디렉토리 규약\n\n```\n.codex/skills/plan-and-build/\n SKILL.md ← 본 파일\n agents/\n 01-initial-plan.md ← 초기기획 sub-agent\n 02-clarify.md ← clarify 9단계 sub-agent\n 03-context-gather.md ← context gather sub-agent\n 04-plan.md ← task/phase 생성 sub-agent\n 05-evaluate.md ← MVP 후 평가 sub-agent\n\nprompts/\n task-created.md ← task/phase 파일 생성 규격 (plan stage가 따름)\n\nscripts/\n run_phases.py ← codex 직렬 실행 runner\n gen_docs_diff.py ← phase 0 후 docs-diff.md 생성\n _utils.py ← 공용 유틸\n\ntasks/\n index.json ← 전체 task 목록\n {id}-{name}/\n index.json ← 이 task의 phase 목록\n artifacts/\n 01-initial-plan.md\n 02-clarify.md\n 03-context.md ← (있을 경우)\n 05-evaluate.md ← (있을 경우)\n prompts/\n clarify/\n 01-feasibility.md ← clarify 9단계 사용자 프롬프트 원문\n 02-tech-stack.md\n ... 09-final-doc.md\n phase0.md\n phase1.md ... phaseN.md\n phase{N}-output.json ← runner가 저장하는 codex 출력\n docs-diff.md ← phase 0 후 자동 생성\n```\n\n## 파이프라인 (5 + 1 stages)\n\n진입 시 사용자 입력 한 줄 요구를 `$INITIAL_BRIEF`로 두고 아래 순서를 따른다.\n각 stage 종료 시 artifact가 디스크에 존재하는지 검증한 뒤 다음 stage로 넘어간다.\n\n### Stage 1. 초기기획 (initial-plan)\n\n`agents/01-initial-plan.md`를 읽고 그 지시대로 진행한다. 사용자와 대화하며 4가지를 확정한다:\n요구사항 / 구현 디테일 / 제약 조건 / 완료 기준.\n\n산출물: `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/01-initial-plan.md`\n\n이 stage가 끝나면 task ID와 name이 결정되어야 한다 (kebab-case slug).\n\n### Stage 2. clarify\n\n`agents/02-clarify.md`를 읽고 9개 sub-step을 순서대로 진행한다:\n\n1. 구현가능성 검증 (feasibility)\n2. 기술스택 선정 (tech-stack)\n3. 사용흐름 점검 (user-flow)\n4. 화면 설계 (ui-design)\n5. API 설계 (api-design)\n6. 데이터 설계 (data-design)\n7. 코드아키텍처 설계 (architecture)\n8. 기술적 결정사항 점검 (tech-decisions)\n9. 최종문서 생성에 대한 논의점 (final-doc)\n\n**자동 기록 의무**: 각 sub-step 시작 시 사용자에게 질문하고, 사용자의 응답을\n받자마자 그 원문을 `tasks/{id}-{name}/prompts/clarify/{NN}-{slug}.md`에 즉시\n저장한다(요약/수정 금지).\n\n산출물: `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/02-clarify.md` (9단계 합의 결과 요약)\n+ `tasks/{id}-{name}/prompts/clarify/01~09-*.md` (사용자 프롬프트 원문)\n\n### Stage 3. context-gather (옵션)\n\n빈 코드베이스이면 **스킵**. 코드베이스 판정:\n\n```\ngit ls-files | wc -l → 0 또는 README/설정 파일만 있으면 빈 코드베이스\n```\n\n스킵 시 `tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/03-context.md`에 한 줄만 적는다:\n`Skipped: empty codebase.`\n\n스킵 안 할 시 `agents/03-context-gather.md` 지시에 따라 explore 한 후 artifact 생성.\n\n### Stage 4. plan\n\n`agents/04-plan.md`를 읽고, 추가로 `prompts/task-created.md`의 규격을 정확히 숙지한다.\n이전 artifact 3개를 모두 input으로 받아 task/phase 파일을 생성한다.\n\n산출물:\n- `tasks/index.json` 업데이트\n- `tasks/{id}-{name}/index.json`\n- `tasks/{id}-{name}/phase0.md` (문서 업데이트 phase)\n- `tasks/{id}-{name}/phase1.md ~ phaseN.md` (구현 phase)\n\n### Stage 5. generate\n\n`scripts/run_phases.py {task-dir}`를 실행한다. orchestrator가 직접 코드를 작성하지 않는다.\nrunner는 각 phase를 독립 codex 세션으로 직렬 실행한다.\n\n```bash\npython scripts/run_phases.py {id}-{name}\n```\n\n실패 시 runner가 종료한다. 사용자에게 실패한 phase의 `index.json` status를 `pending`으로\n되돌리고 재실행하라고 안내한다.\n\n### Stage 6. evaluate (옵션)\n\n간단한 MVP면 **스킵**. 스킵 판정 기준은 `agents/01-initial-plan.md`에서 사용자가 명시한\n\"완료 기준\"을 본다 — 단순 동작 확인 수준이면 스킵.\n\n스킵 안 할 시 `agents/05-evaluate.md`를 따른다. 산출물:\n`tasks/{id}-{name}/artifacts/05-evaluate.md`\n\n## Sub-agent 호출 방식\n\norchestrator(이 SKILL.md를 따르는 IDE 세션)는 각 stage 진입 시:\n\n1. 해당 `agents/0N-*.md` 파일을 Read 도구로 읽는다.\n2. 그 파일이 정의하는 페르소나/입력/출력 스펙을 채택한다.\n3. 입력으로 명시된 이전 artifact들을 모두 Read 한다.\n4. stage 작업을 수행한다 (사용자 인터랙션 또는 분석).\n5. 명시된 artifact를 Write 한다.\n6. artifact 존재 검증 후 다음 stage로.\n\n**plan stage**의 task/phase 파일 생성은 가능하면 별도 codex 서브세션으로 위임할 수 있지만,\n필수는 아니다. orchestrator가 직접 작성해도 무방하다.\n**generate stage**는 항상 `scripts/run_phases.py` subprocess로만 실행한다 (orchestrator가\n구현 코드를 절대 직접 작성하지 않는다).\n\n## 시작 트리거\n\n사용자 입력 예시:\n- \"하네스 시작: <요구사항>\"\n- \"plan-and-build: <요구사항>\"\n- \"이거 만들어줘: <요구사항>\"\n\n이때 orchestrator는 즉시 Stage 1 (`agents/01-initial-plan.md`)로 진입한다.\n\n## 재진입 / 부분 실행\n\n사용자가 특정 stage만 다시 하길 원하면:\n- \"clarify 다시\" → Stage 2부터 재실행, 기존 artifact는 백업 후 덮어씀\n- \"phase 재실행\" → `python scripts/run_phases.py {id}-{name}` 만 호출\n- \"task 만들기만 해줘\" → Stage 4까지만 실행, generate는 사용자가 직접\n\n## 중요 규칙\n\n- artifact 파일을 절대 건너뛰지 말 것. 모든 stage는 명시된 artifact를 생성해야 한다.\n- clarify 사용자 프롬프트는 받자마자 즉시 저장 (대화 종료 후 모아서 저장 금지).\n- task ID는 `tasks/index.json`의 마지막 ID + 1. 처음이면 0.\n- task name은 kebab-case slug, 1~2단어.\n- 모든 timestamp는 ISO 8601 + KST (`+0900`).\n\n succeeded in 859ms:\n\n DocMate는 이 공백을 정확하게 겨냥한다.\n1.2 프로젝트 목표\nDocMate는 정책·장학금 공고 문서를 분석하여 사용자가 즉시 행동할 수 있는 상태로 변환하는 AI 행동 비서이다.\n문서 내 핵심 정보를 구조화된 형식으로 자동 추출\n사용자 프로필과 문서 조건을 비교하여 자격 여부를 3단계로 제시\n실제 행동 단위의 체크리스트 자동 생성\n예외 조건·제한 사항 자동 탐지 및 강조\n신청 페이지 연결, 마감일 캘린더 등록 등 즉시 실행 기능 제공\n2. 경쟁 서비스 분석\n2.1 기존 AI 도구와의 차별점\n아래 비교는 ChatGPT, 뤼튼, Adobe Acrobat AI, 클로바노트 등 현재 시장에서 가장 많이 쓰이는 AI 문서 도구를 기준으로 작성하였다.\n구분\n기존 AI 요약 도구\nDocMate\n핵심 기능\n문서 요약 / 키워드 추출\n행동 체크리스트 자동 생성\n자격 판단\n없음\n프로필 기반 자격 분석\n중요 조건 탐지\n없음 / 부분적\n예외·제한 조건 자동 강조\n행동 연결\n없음\n신청 페이지·서류 발급 직접 연결\n마감 관리\n없음\n캘린더 등록 원클릭\n타겟 도메인\n범용\n정책·장학금 공고 특화 (MVP)\n차별점\n기존 도구는 '요약'에서 멈춘다. DocMate는 요약 이후 행동까지 연결하는 파이프라인 전체를 서비스한다. ChatGPT에 공고문 PDF를 올려 체크리스트를 요청해도, 사용자 프로필 기반 자격 판단·신청 페이지 직접 연결·캘린더 등록은 제공되지 않는다.\n2.2 유사 서비스 사례\n뤼튼 / 클로바X: 범용 문서 Q&A. 사용자가 질문을 직접 설계해야 하며 행동 연결 없음.\nAdobe Acrobat AI: PDF 요약 기능 탑재. 정책 문서 특화 기능 없음.\n정부24 마이페이지: 수혜 자격 정보 제공하나 문서 업로드·체크리스트 기능 없음.\n복지로: 복지 서비스 안내 중심. 문서 분석·행동 연결 기능 없음.\n3. 핵심 기능 (MVP)\n기능 1. 구조화된 핵심 정보 추출\nPDF를 업로드하면 LLM이 아래 항목을 JSON 구조로 자동 추출한다.\n신청 기간 (시작일 / 마감일)\n지원 대상 조건 (나이, 학교, 소득분위, 거주지 등)\n지원 혜택 (금액, 현물, 기간)\n제출 서류 목록\n신청 방법 및 URL\n구현 방식\nPyMuPDF로 PDF 텍스트를 추출한 후, 구조화된 JSON 스키마를 정의하여 Claude API에 Function Calling 방식으로 전달. 출력 결과를 프론트엔드에 카드 형태로 렌더링.\n기능 2. 사용자 프로필 기반 자격 분석\n사용자가 입력한 프로필(나이·학년·거주지·직업·소득분위 등)과 문서에서 추출한 지원 조건을 비교하여 자격 여부를 판정한다.\n🟢 신청 가능: 모든 조건 충족\n🟡 추가 확인 필요: 일부 조건 불확실 (예: 소득분위 미입력)\n🔴 신청 불가: 필수 조건 미충족\n현실적 구현 접근\nMVP 단계에서 조건이 복잡한 문서(다단계 예외, 중첩 조건)는 🟡 '추가 확인 필요'로 보수적 처리. 단순 조건(나이, 학교 소재지, 재학 여부)부터 정확도를 높이고, 고도화 단계에서 복합 조건 파서를 개발한다.\n기능 3. 행동 체크리스트 자동 생성\n문서 내 제출 서류·신청 절차를 '즉시 실행 가능한 할 일' 단위로 변환한다.\n□ 주민등록등본 발급 (정부24 바로가기)\n□ 재학증명서 준비 (학교 포털 바로가기)\n□ 신청 사이트 접속 및 회원가입\n□ 지원서 작성 및 서류 첨부\n□ 마감일 전 최종 제출\n각 항목에는 관련 서류 발급 페이지 URL이 자동으로 연결된다.\n기능 4. 중요 조건 탐지 및 경고\n사용자가 놓치기 쉬운 예외 조건과 제한 사항을 LLM이 자동으로 감지하여 강조 표시한다.\n⚠ 신청 후 내용 수정 불가\n⚠ 동일 사업 중복 지원 시 자동 탈락\n⚠ 서류 미비 시 별도 통보 없이 탈락 처리\n⚠ 외국 국적자 신청 불가\n구현 방식\n프롬프트 내 '주의 조건 탐지' 지시를 별도로 설계하여 위험 신호 키워드(수정 불가, 중복 불가, 자동 탈락, 예외 없음 등)를 추출. 결과를 경고 카드 형태로 상단 고정 표시.\n기능 5. 즉시 실행 연결\n체크리스트 각 항목을 실제 행동과 1:1로 연결하여 앱 내에서 바로 실행 가능하게 한다.\n신청 페이지 이동 버튼 (자동 파싱된 URL)\n관련 서류 발급 페이지 이동 (정부24, 학교 포털 등)\n마감일 Google Calendar 등록 원클릭\n차별성 요약\n기존 AI는 해야 할 일을 알려준다. DocMate는 버튼 하나로 실제 행동까지 연결한다.\n4. 기술 구조\n4.1 AI 파이프라인\nDocMate의 AI 처리 흐름은 3단계로 구성된다.\n문서 파싱: PyMuPDF / pdfplumber로 PDF에서 텍스트 및 레이아웃 추출\nLLM 분석: Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-6)에 구조화된 프롬프트 전달 — 핵심 정보 추출 / 자격 조건 파싱 / 경고 조건 감지 / 체크리스트 생성을 단일 API 호출로 처리\n결과 렌더링: JSON 응답을 프론트엔드 컴포넌트로 파싱하여 카드·체크리스트·경고 배너로 표시\n4.2 기술 스택\n레이어\n기술 / 도구\n역할\n프론트엔드\nReact + TypeScript\nPDF 업로드 UI, 체크리스트 렌더링\n백엔드\nFastAPI (Python)\n문서 처리 파이프라인 API\n문서 파싱\nPyMuPDF / pdfplumber\nPDF 텍스트·구조 추출\nAI 분석\nClaude API (claude-sonnet-4-6)\n핵심 정보 추출 + 조건 분석\n프로필 비교\nRule-based + LLM\n사용자 조건 매칭 로직\n캘린더 연동\nGoogle Calendar API\n마감일 원클릭 등록\n인증\nJWT + OAuth 2.0\n사용자 프로필 저장\n배포\nVercel (FE) + Railway (BE)\n빠른 MVP 배포\n4.3 MVP 범위 정의\n포함\n1~5페이지의 정책·장학금 공고문 / 텍스트 기반 PDF / 대학생·청년 대상 공고\n제외 (고도화 단계)\n복잡한 계약서 / 표·이미지 위주 PDF / 다단계 예외 규칙이 많은 법률 문서\n범위를 제한하는 이유: 정확도 우선 확보 → 사용자 신뢰 형성 → 점진적 확장 전략.\n5. 개발 로드맵\nPhase 1 (1~3주)\nMVP 구현\n• PDF 업로드 및 텍스트 추출 파이프라인\n• Claude API 연동 — 핵심 정보 추출 프롬프트 설계\n• 사용자 프로필 입력 UI (나이·지역·직업·학년)\n• 자격 분석 결과 렌더링 (🟢🟡🔴)\n• 기본 체크리스트 생성 및 표시\nPhase 2 (4~5주)\n기능 고도화\n• 중요 조건 탐지 (예외·제한·중복 신청 금지 등) 강조\n• 신청 페이지 URL 자동 파싱 및 버튼 연결\n• Google Calendar API 연동 — 마감일 등록\n• 문서 유형 자동 분류 (장학금/청년 사업/지자체 공고)\nPhase 3 (6주~)\n검증 & 발표\n• 실제 대학생 10명 대상 사용성 테스트\n• 체크리스트 정확도 측정 (정답 서류 대비 재현율)\n• 자격 분석 오류율 측정 및 프롬프트 개선\n• 발표자료 및 데모 준비\n6. 기대 효과\n6.1 사용자 측면\n문서 검토~신청 완료까지 소요 시간 단축 (예상 50% 이상)\n서류 누락·마감 초과로 인한 신청 실패 감소\n행정 문서에 대한 접근성 향상 — 특히 정보 취약 계층\n6.2 사회적 측면\n청년 복지 수혜율 향상: 인지 대비 신청률 Gap 축소\n행정 서비스 이용 효율 개선\n디지털 포용: 복잡한 행정 언어 장벽 완화\n7. 검증 계획\n7.1 정량 지표\n체크리스트 정확도: 실제 제출 서류 목록 대비 재현율 80% 이상 목표\n자격 분석 정확도: 단순 조건 문서 기준 90% 이상 목표\n사용자 태스크 완료율: 신청 페이지 도달률 70% 이상\n7.2 사용성 테스트 계획\n대상: 재학 중인 대학생 10명 (정보 탐색 경험 다양성 고려)\n방법: 실제 장학금 공고 3종 업로드 후 Think-aloud 프로토콜 적용\n측정: 태스크 완료 시간, 오류 횟수, SUS(시스템 사용성 척도) 설문\n8. 향후 수익 모델\nB2C\n프리미엄 구독: 월 4,900원 — 무제한 문서 분석, 마감 리마인드 알림, 문서 히스토리 저장\n무료 플랜: 월 5회 분석 제한\nB2B / B2G\n대학 공지 시스템 연동 API: 대학 포털에 DocMate 위젯 제공 (기관 라이선스)\n지자체 정책 플랫폼 연동: 정부24·복지로 등 공공 포털 파트너십\n기업 내부 문서 처리: HR 공고·복리후생 안내문 자동 행동화\n수익 모델 우선순위\nMVP 단계에서는 수익화보다 사용자 검증 우선. 월 5회 무료 플랜으로 초기 유저베이스 확보 후, 프리미엄 전환율 측정.\n9. 결론\nDocMate는 'AI가 문서를 요약해준다'는 기존 패러다임에서 벗어나, 'AI가 사용자를 행동하게 만든다'는 새로운 방향을 제시한다.\n기술적 구현 가능성은 이미 검증되어 있다 — PDF 파싱, LLM API 연동, 캘린더 API는 모두 성숙한 기술이다. 남은 과제는 이를 사용자가 실제 체감하는 행동 감소로 이어지게 설계하는 것이다.\nMVP는 대학생 대상 장학금 공고 분석으로 범위를 제한하여 3~4주 내 실제 동작하는 프로토타입을 목표로 한다. 검증 이후 정책 도메인 전체로 확장한다.\n한 줄 요약\nDocMate --git a/docs/adr.md b/docs/adr.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..2b40d57c7ac7f28d7815f19010d4d2822dd3e50d\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/adr.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@\n+# DocMate MVP ADR\n+\n+## ADR-001: Ship A Dependency-Light MVP First\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Use the Python standard library and a no-build frontend as the baseline implementation path.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The execution environment may block package installation.\n+- The project still needs a full demo and test flow without external setup.\n+- Local reliability matters more than framework fidelity in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The current MVP uses `http.server` instead of FastAPI.\n+- The frontend uses plain JavaScript instead of React.\n+- The architecture must preserve upgrade seams for the originally planned stack.\n+\n+## ADR-002: Keep A Deterministic Sample Provider\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Include built-in sample data and deterministic analysis behavior that work without API keys.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product must remain demoable in restricted environments.\n+- Tests should not depend on network access or model variance.\n+- Early product validation is about workflow usefulness, not model-provider sophistication.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sample outputs must be stable across runs.\n+- Real LLM integrations remain optional extension points.\n+\n+## ADR-003: Prefer Conservative Eligibility Outcomes\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Return `needs_review` whenever conditions are incomplete, ambiguous, or too complex for safe rule evaluation.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- A false \"eligible\" result is more harmful than asking the user to verify a condition.\n+- Scholarship and policy announcements often contain exceptions and edge cases.\n+- The MVP only guarantees high confidence on simple rule patterns.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The product will intentionally avoid over-automation for complex documents.\n+- Missing profile fields are treated as unresolved, not ignored.\n+\n+## ADR-004: Make The First Screen The Working Tool\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+The initial UI must open directly into the analysis workspace rather than a marketing landing page.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product value is demonstrated through action, not explanation.\n+- The user flow starts with upload, profile input, and result inspection.\n+- The MVP should minimize clicks between arrival and analysis.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The frontend prioritizes form and result density over promotional content.\n+- Empty, loading, error, and result states are product-critical.\n+\n+## ADR-005: Exclude Persistence And OAuth From The MVP\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Do not implement database storage, login, or real calendar write access in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- These features add setup and operational complexity without validating the core insight.\n+- The immediate product question is whether action-oriented document analysis is useful.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sessions are ephemeral.\n+- Calendar support is limited to links or generated artifacts rather than direct account integration.\n+- User intervention for keys and OAuth remains documented separately.\ndiff --git a/docs/architecture.md b/docs/architecture.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..d0a65eca9e73611db452598bdff74acafe459395\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/architecture.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,183 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Architecture\n+\n+## Architecture Summary\n+\n+The implemented MVP uses a dependency-light local architecture:\n+\n+- Python backend with standard-library HTTP serving\n+- Static no-build frontend\n+- Deterministic analysis pipeline for demo and tests\n+- Clear extension points for future FastAPI, React, and external LLM providers\n+\n+This is intentionally narrower than the long-term proposal. The goal of the current architecture is local reliability first.\n+\n+## System Components\n+\n+### Frontend\n+\n+- `frontend/index.html`\n+ - Single-screen work surface\n+ - Loads local JavaScript and CSS only\n+- `frontend/src/app.js`\n+ - Handles upload, profile entry, sample loading, analysis requests, and result rendering\n+- `frontend/src/styles.css`\n+ - Presents an operator-style tool UI optimized for scanning and action-taking\n+\n+Responsibilities:\n+\n+- Collect document and profile input\n+- Call backend APIs\n+- Render eligibility, extraction, warnings, checklist, and action links\n+- Fall back to sample data when backend access is unavailable\n+\n+### Backend\n+\n+- `backend/app/models.py`\n+ - Core data structures for profile, extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and analysis response\n+- `backend/app/parser.py`\n+ - Converts uploaded content into text\n+ - Handles safe PDF fallback behavior\n+- `backend/app/analyzer.py`\n+ - Produces structured extraction and warning candidates\n+ - Coordinates sample provider behavior\n+- `backend/app/eligibility.py`\n+ - Applies conservative rule-based eligibility evaluation\n+- `backend/app/checklist.py`\n+ - Converts extracted requirements into actionable checklist items\n+- `backend/app/sample_data.py`\n+ - Supplies deterministic sample announcement text and expected profile/result data\n+- `backend/app/server.py`\n+ - Exposes HTTP endpoints and static file serving\n+- `backend/run.py`\n+ - Starts the local server\n+\n+### Tests\n+\n+- `tests/backend/test_analysis.py`\n+ - Verifies pipeline behavior and rule outcomes\n+- `tests/backend/test_server.py`\n+ - Verifies HTTP routes and payload handling\n+\n+## Runtime Flow\n+\n+1. The user opens the frontend from the same server that exposes the API.\n+2. The frontend requests `GET /api/sample` for the sample workflow or submits `POST /api/analyze`.\n+3. The backend parses the uploaded content into text.\n+4. The analyzer extracts structured fields and warning candidates.\n+5. The eligibility module evaluates the extraction against the profile.\n+6. The checklist module turns extracted requirements into action items.\n+7. The backend returns a single structured analysis response to the frontend.\n+\n+Supporting route:\n+\n+- `GET /health` returns a lightweight status check.\n+\n+## API Shape\n+\n+### `GET /health`\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"status\": \"ok\" }\n+```\n+\n+### `GET /api/sample`\n+\n+Response includes:\n+\n+- Sample source text\n+- Sample profile\n+- Sample analysis result or enough data to build one deterministically\n+\n+### `POST /api/analyze`\n+\n+Request:\n+\n+- Multipart form data or another dependency-light format carrying:\n+ - uploaded file content or raw text\n+ - profile fields\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+ \"extraction\": {},\n+ \"eligibility\": {},\n+ \"warnings\": [],\n+ \"checklist\": [],\n+ \"actions\": []\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Errors must use a simple JSON shape such as:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"detail\": \"...\" }\n+```\n+\n+## Data Model Overview\n+\n+### Profile\n+\n+- `age`\n+- `grade`\n+- `region`\n+- `occupation`\n+- `income_decile`\n+- `enrolled`\n+\n+### DocumentExtraction\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `application_period`\n+- `eligibility_conditions`\n+- `benefits`\n+- `required_documents`\n+- `application_method`\n+- `application_url`\n+\n+### EligibilityResult\n+\n+- `status`\n+- `reasons`\n+- `missing_information`\n+\n+### WarningItem\n+\n+- `severity`\n+- `title`\n+- `evidence`\n+\n+### ChecklistItem\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `description`\n+- `action_url`\n+- `done`\n+\n+### AnalysisResult\n+\n+- `extraction`\n+- `eligibility`\n+- `warnings`\n+- `checklist`\n+- `actions`\n+\n+## Design Constraints\n+\n+- No persistent database in the MVP\n+- No external network requirement for tests or demo\n+- No dependency on FastAPI, React, or third-party PDF parsing libraries for the base flow\n+- No file persistence for user uploads\n+\n+## Extension Path\n+\n+The architecture keeps explicit upgrade points:\n+\n+- Replace the standard-library server with FastAPI when dependency freedom is no longer required.\n+- Replace the no-build frontend with React + TypeScript when a richer component model is needed.\n+- Add provider adapters for Claude or OpenAI after API key setup is available.\n+- Add Google Calendar OAuth once the MVP proves user value.\n+\n+These are planned extensions, not current requirements.\ndiff --git a/docs/spec.md b/docs/spec.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..161c4fc8fb709312b1075f0566214e3812d70bab\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/spec.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,122 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Spec\n+\n+## Product Goal\n+\n+DocMate turns scholarship and youth-policy announcement documents into action-ready guidance.\n+The MVP must help a user move from \"I found a document\" to \"I know whether I can apply and what to do next\" without requiring an API key or external service setup.\n+\n+Core outcomes:\n+\n+- Extract the key application details from an uploaded document.\n+- Compare those details against a user profile and return a conservative 3-state eligibility result.\n+- Surface warnings that commonly cause failed applications.\n+- Convert requirements into a concrete checklist with direct action links.\n+- Provide immediate next actions such as opening the application page and adding a deadline to a calendar flow.\n+\n+## MVP Scope\n+\n+### Supported Inputs\n+\n+- Text-based PDF documents, primarily 1 to 5 pages.\n+- Plain text uploads or decodable file content for local testing and fallback flows.\n+- Scholarship and youth-policy announcements for university students and young adults.\n+\n+### Supported User Profile Fields\n+\n+- Age\n+- Grade\n+- Region\n+- Occupation\n+- Income decile\n+- Enrollment status\n+\n+### Supported Outputs\n+\n+- Structured summary of the announcement:\n+ - Title\n+ - Application period\n+ - Eligibility conditions\n+ - Benefits\n+ - Required documents\n+ - Application method\n+ - Application URL\n+- Eligibility result:\n+ - `eligible`\n+ - `needs_review`\n+ - `ineligible`\n+- Warning items for risky conditions such as:\n+ - Duplicate application restrictions\n+ - No-edit policies\n+ - Automatic rejection conditions\n+ - Missing-document rejection\n+ - Nationality restrictions\n+- Actionable checklist items with optional related URLs.\n+- Immediate actions for opening the application page and registering the deadline.\n+\n+## Primary User Flow\n+\n+1. The user uploads a document or chooses a built-in sample announcement.\n+2. The user enters profile information.\n+3. The system extracts document text.\n+4. The system analyzes the text into a structured extraction.\n+5. The system evaluates eligibility against the profile.\n+6. The system highlights warnings and generates a checklist.\n+7. The user follows the provided action links.\n+\n+## Functional Requirements\n+\n+### Document Analysis\n+\n+- The system must accept uploaded file content without permanently storing the original file on disk.\n+- The system must extract text from decodable inputs directly.\n+- The system must handle PDFs safely even when a PDF parsing library is unavailable.\n+- The system must preserve a deterministic sample flow so the MVP demo works without external dependencies.\n+\n+### Eligibility Evaluation\n+\n+- The system must use rule-based checks for simple conditions such as age, region, and enrollment.\n+- The system must return `needs_review` when profile data is missing or the condition cannot be interpreted confidently.\n+- The system must avoid optimistic guesses for complex nested conditions.\n+\n+### Warning Detection\n+\n+- The system must detect and surface critical risk language when present in the source text.\n+- The warning list must prioritize issues that can cause submission failure or disqualification.\n+\n+### Checklist Generation\n+\n+- The system must convert required documents and application steps into action-oriented checklist items.\n+- Checklist items should use imperative phrasing so the user can act immediately.\n+\n+### Demo Reliability\n+\n+- The MVP must return a consistent sample analysis result without requiring:\n+ - API keys\n+ - OAuth setup\n+ - database provisioning\n+ - external network access\n+\n+## Non-Goals\n+\n+The MVP explicitly excludes:\n+\n+- Login, subscription, billing, or account management\n+- Persistent database storage and history\n+- Real Google OAuth write access\n+- Automatic document issuance from government or school portals\n+- Image-heavy PDFs and OCR-first pipelines\n+- Legal or contract documents with dense nested exceptions\n+- Full automation for complex eligibility rules\n+\n+## Implementation Constraints\n+\n+- The local MVP must be dependency-light and runnable in restricted environments.\n+- The backend must work with the Python standard library only for the core demo path.\n+- The frontend must render without npm install or external CDN assets.\n+- Real LLM providers and richer frameworks remain extension points, not MVP requirements.\n+\n+## Definition Of Done For The MVP\n+\n+- A user can complete the sample analysis flow end to end on a local machine without network access.\n+- Structured extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and action links are all visible in the product flow.\n+- The implementation remains aligned with the future path toward FastAPI, React, and real LLM providers, but does not depend on them now.\ndiff --git a/docs/user-intervention.md b/docs/user-intervention.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..3c0499d292837c5b05151a792142d8f6311fc57e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/user-intervention.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@\n+# DocMate MVP User Intervention\n+\n+## Current MVP Requirement\n+\n+No user-managed API key, OAuth configuration, or external service account is required for the local MVP demo and test flow.\n+\n+The intended baseline experience is:\n+\n+1. Start the local server.\n+2. Open the app.\n+3. Upload a supported file or use the sample announcement.\n+4. Review the generated analysis.\n+\n+## Manual Actions The User May Still Perform\n+\n+- Choose a sample announcement instead of uploading a document when testing fallback behavior.\n+- Verify final application details against the original announcement before submitting to the real institution.\n+- Open external application or document-issuance links provided by the result screen.\n+\n+## Not Required In This Phase\n+\n+The following are intentionally not required for MVP completion:\n+\n+- Claude or OpenAI API key setup\n+- Google Calendar OAuth\n+- Database provisioning\n+- Cloud deployment credentials\n+\n+## Future Optional Setup\n+\n+These setup steps may be introduced after the dependency-light MVP is validated:\n+\n+- `DOCMATE_LLM_PROVIDER` and provider-specific API key variables for real document analysis\n+- Google OAuth client configuration for direct calendar event creation\n+- Institution-specific link maps for school portals or government issuance sites\n+\n+If these future integrations are added, their setup instructions should stay in this document rather than being mixed into the core product spec.\n\ndiff --git a/docs/adr.md b/docs/adr.md\nnew file mode 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..161c4fc8fb709312b1075f0566214e3812d70bab\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/spec.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,122 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Spec\n+\n+## Product Goal\n+\n+DocMate turns scholarship and youth-policy announcement documents into action-ready guidance.\n+The MVP must help a user move from \"I found a document\" to \"I know whether I can apply and what to do next\" without requiring an API key or external service setup.\n+\n+Core outcomes:\n+\n+- Extract the key application details from an uploaded document.\n+- Compare those details against a user profile and return a conservative 3-state eligibility result.\n+- Surface warnings that commonly cause failed applications.\n+- Convert requirements into a concrete checklist with direct action links.\n+- Provide immediate next actions such as opening the application page and adding a deadline to a calendar flow.\n+\n+## MVP Scope\n+\n+### Supported Inputs\n+\n+- Text-based PDF documents, primarily 1 to 5 pages.\n+- Plain text uploads or decodable file content for local testing and fallback flows.\n+- Scholarship and youth-policy announcements for university students and young adults.\n+\n+### Supported User Profile Fields\n+\n+- Age\n+- Grade\n+- Region\n+- Occupation\n+- Income decile\n+- Enrollment status\n+\n+### Supported Outputs\n+\n+- Structured summary of the announcement:\n+ - Title\n+ - Application period\n+ - Eligibility conditions\n+ - Benefits\n+ - Required documents\n+ - Application method\n+ - Application URL\n+- Eligibility result:\n+ - `eligible`\n+ - `needs_review`\n+ - `ineligible`\n+- Warning items for risky conditions such as:\n+ - Duplicate application restrictions\n+ - No-edit policies\n+ - Automatic rejection conditions\n+ - Missing-document rejection\n+ - Nationality restrictions\n+- Actionable checklist items with optional related URLs.\n+- Immediate actions for opening the application page and registering the deadline.\n+\n+## Primary User Flow\n+\n+1. The user uploads a document or chooses a built-in sample announcement.\n+2. The user enters profile information.\n+3. The system extracts document text.\n+4. The system analyzes the text into a structured extraction.\n+5. The system evaluates eligibility against the profile.\n+6. The system highlights warnings and generates a checklist.\n+7. The user follows the provided action links.\n+\n+## Functional Requirements\n+\n+### Document Analysis\n+\n+- The system must accept uploaded file content without permanently storing the original file on disk.\n+- The system must extract text from decodable inputs directly.\n+- The system must handle PDFs safely even when a PDF parsing library is unavailable.\n+- The system must preserve a deterministic sample flow so the MVP demo works without external dependencies.\n+\n+### Eligibility Evaluation\n+\n+- The system must use rule-based checks for simple conditions such as age, region, and enrollment.\n+- The system must return `needs_review` when profile data is missing or the condition cannot be interpreted confidently.\n+- The system must avoid optimistic guesses for complex nested conditions.\n+\n+### Warning Detection\n+\n+- The system must detect and surface critical risk language when present in the source text.\n+- The warning list must prioritize issues that can cause submission failure or disqualification.\n+\n+### Checklist Generation\n+\n+- The system must convert required documents and application steps into action-oriented checklist items.\n+- Checklist items should use imperative phrasing so the user can act immediately.\n+\n+### Demo Reliability\n+\n+- The MVP must return a consistent sample analysis result without requiring:\n+ - API keys\n+ - OAuth setup\n+ - database provisioning\n+ - external network access\n+\n+## Non-Goals\n+\n+The MVP explicitly excludes:\n+\n+- Login, subscription, billing, or account management\n+- Persistent database storage and history\n+- Real Google OAuth write access\n+- Automatic document issuance from government or school portals\n+- Image-heavy PDFs and OCR-first pipelines\n+- Legal or contract documents with dense nested exceptions\n+- Full automation for complex eligibility rules\n+\n+## Implementation Constraints\n+\n+- The local MVP must be dependency-light and runnable in restricted environments.\n+- The backend must work with the Python standard library only for the core demo path.\n+- The frontend must render without npm install or external CDN assets.\n+- Real LLM providers and richer frameworks remain extension points, not MVP requirements.\n+\n+## Definition Of Done For The MVP\n+\n+- A user can complete the sample analysis flow end to end on a local machine without network access.\n+- Structured extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and action links are all visible in the product flow.\n+- The implementation remains aligned with the future path toward FastAPI, React, and real LLM providers, but does not depend on them now.\ndiff --git a/docs/user-intervention.md b/docs/user-intervention.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..3c0499d292837c5b05151a792142d8f6311fc57e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/user-intervention.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@\n+# DocMate MVP User Intervention\n+\n+## Current MVP Requirement\n+\n+No user-managed API key, OAuth configuration, or external service account is required for the local MVP demo and test flow.\n+\n+The intended baseline experience is:\n+\n+1. Start the local server.\n+2. Open the app.\n+3. Upload a supported file or use the sample announcement.\n+4. Review the generated analysis.\n+\n+## Manual Actions The User May Still Perform\n+\n+- Choose a sample announcement instead of uploading a document when testing fallback behavior.\n+- Verify final application details against the original announcement before submitting to the real institution.\n+- Open external application or document-issuance links provided by the result screen.\n+\n+## Not Required In This Phase\n+\n+The following are intentionally not required for MVP completion:\n+\n+- Claude or OpenAI API key setup\n+- Google Calendar OAuth\n+- Database provisioning\n+- Cloud deployment credentials\n+\n+## Future Optional Setup\n+\n+These setup steps may be introduced after the dependency-light MVP is validated:\n+\n+- `DOCMATE_LLM_PROVIDER` and provider-specific API key variables for real document analysis\n+- Google OAuth client configuration for direct calendar event creation\n+- Institution-specific link maps for school portals or government issuance sites\n+\n+If these future integrations are added, their setup instructions should stay in this document rather than being mixed into the core product spec.\n\n2026-05-19T01:00:05.981439Z WARN 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..2b40d57c7ac7f28d7815f19010d4d2822dd3e50d\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/adr.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@\n+# DocMate MVP ADR\n+\n+## ADR-001: Ship A Dependency-Light MVP First\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Use the Python standard library and a no-build frontend as the baseline implementation path.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The execution environment may block package installation.\n+- The project still needs a full demo and test flow without external setup.\n+- Local reliability matters more than framework fidelity in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The current MVP uses `http.server` instead of FastAPI.\n+- The frontend uses plain JavaScript instead of React.\n+- The architecture must preserve upgrade seams for the originally planned stack.\n+\n+## ADR-002: Keep A Deterministic Sample Provider\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Include built-in sample data and deterministic analysis behavior that work without API keys.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product must remain demoable in restricted environments.\n+- Tests should not depend on network access or model variance.\n+- Early product validation is about workflow usefulness, not model-provider sophistication.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sample outputs must be stable across runs.\n+- Real LLM integrations remain optional extension points.\n+\n+## ADR-003: Prefer Conservative Eligibility Outcomes\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Return `needs_review` whenever conditions are incomplete, ambiguous, or too complex for safe rule evaluation.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- A false \"eligible\" result is more harmful than asking the user to verify a condition.\n+- Scholarship and policy announcements often contain exceptions and edge cases.\n+- The MVP only guarantees high confidence on simple rule patterns.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The product will intentionally avoid over-automation for complex documents.\n+- Missing profile fields are treated as unresolved, not ignored.\n+\n+## ADR-004: Make The First Screen The Working Tool\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+The initial UI must open directly into the analysis workspace rather than a marketing landing page.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product value is demonstrated through action, not explanation.\n+- The user flow starts with upload, profile input, and result inspection.\n+- The MVP should minimize clicks between arrival and analysis.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The frontend prioritizes form and result density over promotional content.\n+- Empty, loading, error, and result states are product-critical.\n+\n+## ADR-005: Exclude Persistence And OAuth From The MVP\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Do not implement database storage, login, or real calendar write access in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- These features add setup and operational complexity without validating the core insight.\n+- The immediate product question is whether action-oriented document analysis is useful.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sessions are ephemeral.\n+- Calendar support is limited to links or generated artifacts rather than direct account integration.\n+- User intervention for keys and OAuth remains documented separately.\ndiff --git a/docs/architecture.md b/docs/architecture.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..d0a65eca9e73611db452598bdff74acafe459395\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/architecture.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,183 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Architecture\n+\n+## Architecture Summary\n+\n+The implemented MVP uses a dependency-light local architecture:\n+\n+- Python backend with standard-library HTTP serving\n+- Static no-build frontend\n+- Deterministic analysis pipeline for demo and tests\n+- Clear extension points for future FastAPI, React, and external LLM providers\n+\n+This is intentionally narrower than the long-term proposal. The goal of the current architecture is local reliability first.\n+\n+## System Components\n+\n+### Frontend\n+\n+- `frontend/index.html`\n+ - Single-screen work surface\n+ - Loads local JavaScript and CSS only\n+- `frontend/src/app.js`\n+ - Handles upload, profile entry, sample loading, analysis requests, and result rendering\n+- `frontend/src/styles.css`\n+ - Presents an operator-style tool UI optimized for scanning and action-taking\n+\n+Responsibilities:\n+\n+- Collect document and profile input\n+- Call backend APIs\n+- Render eligibility, extraction, warnings, checklist, and action links\n+- Fall back to sample data when backend access is unavailable\n+\n+### Backend\n+\n+- `backend/app/models.py`\n+ - Core data structures for profile, extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and analysis response\n+- `backend/app/parser.py`\n+ - Converts uploaded content into text\n+ - Handles safe PDF fallback behavior\n+- `backend/app/analyzer.py`\n+ - Produces structured extraction and warning candidates\n+ - Coordinates sample provider behavior\n+- `backend/app/eligibility.py`\n+ - Applies conservative rule-based eligibility evaluation\n+- `backend/app/checklist.py`\n+ - Converts extracted requirements into actionable checklist items\n+- `backend/app/sample_data.py`\n+ - Supplies deterministic sample announcement text and expected profile/result data\n+- `backend/app/server.py`\n+ - Exposes HTTP endpoints and static file serving\n+- `backend/run.py`\n+ - Starts the local server\n+\n+### Tests\n+\n+- `tests/backend/test_analysis.py`\n+ - Verifies pipeline behavior and rule outcomes\n+- `tests/backend/test_server.py`\n+ - Verifies HTTP routes and payload handling\n+\n+## Runtime Flow\n+\n+1. The user opens the frontend from the same server that exposes the API.\n+2. The frontend requests `GET /api/sample` for the sample workflow or submits `POST /api/analyze`.\n+3. The backend parses the uploaded content into text.\n+4. The analyzer extracts structured fields and warning candidates.\n+5. The eligibility module evaluates the extraction against the profile.\n+6. The checklist module turns extracted requirements into action items.\n+7. The backend returns a single structured analysis response to the frontend.\n+\n+Supporting route:\n+\n+- `GET /health` returns a lightweight status check.\n+\n+## API Shape\n+\n+### `GET /health`\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"status\": \"ok\" }\n+```\n+\n+### `GET /api/sample`\n+\n+Response includes:\n+\n+- Sample source text\n+- Sample profile\n+- Sample analysis result or enough data to build one deterministically\n+\n+### `POST /api/analyze`\n+\n+Request:\n+\n+- Multipart form data or another dependency-light format carrying:\n+ - uploaded file content or raw text\n+ - profile fields\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+ \"extraction\": {},\n+ \"eligibility\": {},\n+ \"warnings\": [],\n+ \"checklist\": [],\n+ \"actions\": []\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Errors must use a simple JSON shape such as:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"detail\": \"...\" }\n+```\n+\n+## Data Model Overview\n+\n+### Profile\n+\n+- `age`\n+- `grade`\n+- `region`\n+- `occupation`\n+- `income_decile`\n+- `enrolled`\n+\n+### DocumentExtraction\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `application_period`\n+- `eligibility_conditions`\n+- `benefits`\n+- `required_documents`\n+- `application_method`\n+- `application_url`\n+\n+### EligibilityResult\n+\n+- `status`\n+- `reasons`\n+- `missing_information`\n+\n+### WarningItem\n+\n+- `severity`\n+- `title`\n+- `evidence`\n+\n+### ChecklistItem\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `description`\n+- `action_url`\n+- `done`\n+\n+### AnalysisResult\n+\n+- `extraction`\n+- `eligibility`\n+- `warnings`\n+- `checklist`\n+- `actions`\n+\n+## Design Constraints\n+\n+- No persistent database in the MVP\n+- No external network requirement for tests or demo\n+- No dependency on FastAPI, React, or third-party PDF parsing libraries for the base flow\n+- No file persistence for user uploads\n+\n+## Extension Path\n+\n+The architecture keeps explicit upgrade points:\n+\n+- Replace the standard-library server with FastAPI when dependency freedom is no longer required.\n+- Replace the no-build frontend with React + TypeScript when a richer component model is needed.\n+- Add provider adapters for Claude or OpenAI after API key setup is available.\n+- Add Google Calendar OAuth once the MVP proves user value.\n+\n+These are planned extensions, not current requirements.\ndiff --git a/docs/spec.md b/docs/spec.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..2b40d57c7ac7f28d7815f19010d4d2822dd3e50d\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/adr.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@\n+# DocMate MVP ADR\n+\n+## ADR-001: Ship A Dependency-Light MVP First\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Use the Python standard library and a no-build frontend as the baseline implementation path.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The execution environment may block package installation.\n+- The project still needs a full demo and test flow without external setup.\n+- Local reliability matters more than framework fidelity in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The current MVP uses `http.server` instead of FastAPI.\n+- The frontend uses plain JavaScript instead of React.\n+- The architecture must preserve upgrade seams for the originally planned stack.\n+\n+## ADR-002: Keep A Deterministic Sample Provider\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Include built-in sample data and deterministic analysis behavior that work without API keys.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product must remain demoable in restricted environments.\n+- Tests should not depend on network access or model variance.\n+- Early product validation is about workflow usefulness, not model-provider sophistication.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sample outputs must be stable across runs.\n+- Real LLM integrations remain optional extension points.\n+\n+## ADR-003: Prefer Conservative Eligibility Outcomes\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Return `needs_review` whenever conditions are incomplete, ambiguous, or too complex for safe rule evaluation.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- A false \"eligible\" result is more harmful than asking the user to verify a condition.\n+- Scholarship and policy announcements often contain exceptions and edge cases.\n+- The MVP only guarantees high confidence on simple rule patterns.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The product will intentionally avoid over-automation for complex documents.\n+- Missing profile fields are treated as unresolved, not ignored.\n+\n+## ADR-004: Make The First Screen The Working Tool\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+The initial UI must open directly into the analysis workspace rather than a marketing landing page.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product value is demonstrated through action, not explanation.\n+- The user flow starts with upload, profile input, and result inspection.\n+- The MVP should minimize clicks between arrival and analysis.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The frontend prioritizes form and result density over promotional content.\n+- Empty, loading, error, and result states are product-critical.\n+\n+## ADR-005: Exclude Persistence And OAuth From The MVP\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Do not implement database storage, login, or real calendar write access in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- These features add setup and operational complexity without validating the core insight.\n+- The immediate product question is whether action-oriented document analysis is useful.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sessions are ephemeral.\n+- Calendar support is limited to links or generated artifacts rather than direct account integration.\n+- User intervention for keys and OAuth remains documented separately.\ndiff --git a/docs/architecture.md b/docs/architecture.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..d0a65eca9e73611db452598bdff74acafe459395\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/architecture.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,183 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Architecture\n+\n+## Architecture Summary\n+\n+The implemented MVP uses a dependency-light local architecture:\n+\n+- Python backend with standard-library HTTP serving\n+- Static no-build frontend\n+- Deterministic analysis pipeline for demo and tests\n+- Clear extension points for future FastAPI, React, and external LLM providers\n+\n+This is intentionally narrower than the long-term proposal. The goal of the current architecture is local reliability first.\n+\n+## System Components\n+\n+### Frontend\n+\n+- `frontend/index.html`\n+ - Single-screen work surface\n+ - Loads local JavaScript and CSS only\n+- `frontend/src/app.js`\n+ - Handles upload, profile entry, sample loading, analysis requests, and result rendering\n+- `frontend/src/styles.css`\n+ - Presents an operator-style tool UI optimized for scanning and action-taking\n+\n+Responsibilities:\n+\n+- Collect document and profile input\n+- Call backend APIs\n+- Render eligibility, extraction, warnings, checklist, and action links\n+- Fall back to sample data when backend access is unavailable\n+\n+### Backend\n+\n+- `backend/app/models.py`\n+ - Core data structures for profile, extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and analysis response\n+- `backend/app/parser.py`\n+ - Converts uploaded content into text\n+ - Handles safe PDF fallback behavior\n+- `backend/app/analyzer.py`\n+ - Produces structured extraction and warning candidates\n+ - Coordinates sample provider behavior\n+- `backend/app/eligibility.py`\n+ - Applies conservative rule-based eligibility evaluation\n+- `backend/app/checklist.py`\n+ - Converts extracted requirements into actionable checklist items\n+- `backend/app/sample_data.py`\n+ - Supplies deterministic sample announcement text and expected profile/result data\n+- `backend/app/server.py`\n+ - Exposes HTTP endpoints and static file serving\n+- `backend/run.py`\n+ - Starts the local server\n+\n+### Tests\n+\n+- `tests/backend/test_analysis.py`\n+ - Verifies pipeline behavior and rule outcomes\n+- `tests/backend/test_server.py`\n+ - Verifies HTTP routes and payload handling\n+\n+## Runtime Flow\n+\n+1. The user opens the frontend from the same server that exposes the API.\n+2. The frontend requests `GET /api/sample` for the sample workflow or submits `POST /api/analyze`.\n+3. The backend parses the uploaded content into text.\n+4. The analyzer extracts structured fields and warning candidates.\n+5. The eligibility module evaluates the extraction against the profile.\n+6. The checklist module turns extracted requirements into action items.\n+7. The backend returns a single structured analysis response to the frontend.\n+\n+Supporting route:\n+\n+- `GET /health` returns a lightweight status check.\n+\n+## API Shape\n+\n+### `GET /health`\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"status\": \"ok\" }\n+```\n+\n+### `GET /api/sample`\n+\n+Response includes:\n+\n+- Sample source text\n+- Sample profile\n+- Sample analysis result or enough data to build one deterministically\n+\n+### `POST /api/analyze`\n+\n+Request:\n+\n+- Multipart form data or another dependency-light format carrying:\n+ - uploaded file content or raw text\n+ - profile fields\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+ \"extraction\": {},\n+ \"eligibility\": {},\n+ \"warnings\": [],\n+ \"checklist\": [],\n+ \"actions\": []\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Errors must use a simple JSON shape such as:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"detail\": \"...\" }\n+```\n+\n+## Data Model Overview\n+\n+### Profile\n+\n+- `age`\n+- `grade`\n+- `region`\n+- `occupation`\n+- `income_decile`\n+- `enrolled`\n+\n+### DocumentExtraction\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `application_period`\n+- `eligibility_conditions`\n+- `benefits`\n+- `required_documents`\n+- `application_method`\n+- `application_url`\n+\n+### EligibilityResult\n+\n+- `status`\n+- `reasons`\n+- `missing_information`\n+\n+### WarningItem\n+\n+- `severity`\n+- `title`\n+- `evidence`\n+\n+### ChecklistItem\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `description`\n+- `action_url`\n+- `done`\n+\n+### AnalysisResult\n+\n+- `extraction`\n+- `eligibility`\n+- `warnings`\n+- `checklist`\n+- `actions`\n+\n+## Design Constraints\n+\n+- No persistent database in the MVP\n+- No external network requirement for tests or demo\n+- No dependency on FastAPI, React, or third-party PDF parsing libraries for the base flow\n+- No file persistence for user uploads\n+\n+## Extension Path\n+\n+The architecture keeps explicit upgrade points:\n+\n+- Replace the standard-library server with FastAPI when dependency freedom is no longer required.\n+- Replace the no-build frontend with React + TypeScript when a richer component model is needed.\n+- Add provider adapters for Claude or OpenAI after API key setup is available.\n+- Add Google Calendar OAuth once the MVP proves user value.\n+\n+These are planned extensions, not current requirements.\ndiff --git a/docs/spec.md b/docs/spec.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..161c4fc8fb709312b1075f0566214e3812d70bab\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/spec.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,122 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Spec\n+\n+## Product Goal\n+\n+DocMate turns scholarship and youth-policy announcement documents into action-ready guidance.\n+The MVP must help a user move from \"I found a document\" to \"I know whether I can apply and what to do next\" without requiring an API key or external service setup.\n+\n+Core outcomes:\n+\n+- Extract the key application details from an uploaded document.\n+- Compare those details against a user profile and return a conservative 3-state eligibility result.\n+- Surface warnings that commonly cause failed applications.\n+- Convert requirements into a concrete checklist with direct action links.\n+- Provide immediate next actions such as opening the application page and adding a deadline to a calendar flow.\n+\n+## MVP Scope\n+\n+### Supported Inputs\n+\n+- Text-based PDF documents, primarily 1 to 5 pages.\n+- Plain text uploads or decodable file content for local testing and fallback flows.\n+- Scholarship and youth-policy announcements for university students and young adults.\n+\n+### Supported User Profile Fields\n+\n+- Age\n+- Grade\n+- Region\n+- Occupation\n+- Income decile\n+- Enrollment status\n+\n+### Supported Outputs\n+\n+- Structured summary of the announcement:\n+ - Title\n+ - Application period\n+ - Eligibility conditions\n+ - Benefits\n+ - Required documents\n+ - Application method\n+ - Application URL\n+- Eligibility result:\n+ - `eligible`\n+ - `needs_review`\n+ - `ineligible`\n+- Warning items for risky conditions such as:\n+ - Duplicate application restrictions\n+ - No-edit policies\n+ - Automatic rejection conditions\n+ - Missing-document rejection\n+ - Nationality restrictions\n+- Actionable checklist items with optional related URLs.\n+- Immediate actions for opening the application page and registering the deadline.\n+\n+## Primary User Flow\n+\n+1. The user uploads a document or chooses a built-in sample announcement.\n+2. The user enters profile information.\n+3. The system extracts document text.\n+4. The system analyzes the text into a structured extraction.\n+5. The system evaluates eligibility against the profile.\n+6. The system highlights warnings and generates a checklist.\n+7. The user follows the provided action links.\n+\n+## Functional Requirements\n+\n+### Document Analysis\n+\n+- The system must accept uploaded file content without permanently storing the original file on disk.\n+- The system must extract text from decodable inputs directly.\n+- The system must handle PDFs safely even when a PDF parsing library is unavailable.\n+- The system must preserve a deterministic sample flow so the MVP demo works without external dependencies.\n+\n+### Eligibility Evaluation\n+\n+- The system must use rule-based checks for simple conditions such as age, region, and enrollment.\n+- The system must return `needs_review` when profile data is missing or the condition cannot be interpreted confidently.\n+- The system must avoid optimistic guesses for complex nested conditions.\n+\n+### Warning Detection\n+\n+- The system must detect and surface critical risk language when present in the source text.\n+- The warning list must prioritize issues that can cause submission failure or disqualification.\n+\n+### Checklist Generation\n+\n+- The system must convert required documents and application steps into action-oriented checklist items.\n+- Checklist items should use imperative phrasing so the user can act immediately.\n+\n+### Demo Reliability\n+\n+- The MVP must return a consistent sample analysis result without requiring:\n+ - API keys\n+ - OAuth setup\n+ - database provisioning\n+ - external network access\n+\n+## Non-Goals\n+\n+The MVP explicitly excludes:\n+\n+- Login, subscription, billing, or account management\n+- Persistent database storage and history\n+- Real Google OAuth write access\n+- Automatic document issuance from government or school portals\n+- Image-heavy PDFs and OCR-first pipelines\n+- Legal or contract documents with dense nested exceptions\n+- Full automation for complex eligibility rules\n+\n+## Implementation Constraints\n+\n+- The local MVP must be dependency-light and runnable in restricted environments.\n+- The backend must work with the Python standard library only for the core demo path.\n+- The frontend must render without npm install or external CDN assets.\n+- Real LLM providers and richer frameworks remain extension points, not MVP requirements.\n+\n+## Definition Of Done For The MVP\n+\n+- A user can complete the sample analysis flow end to end on a local machine without network access.\n+- Structured extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and action links are all visible in the product flow.\n+- The implementation remains aligned with the future path toward FastAPI, React, and real LLM providers, but does not depend on them now.\ndiff --git a/docs/user-intervention.md b/docs/user-intervention.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..3c0499d292837c5b05151a792142d8f6311fc57e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/user-intervention.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@\n+# DocMate MVP User Intervention\n+\n+## Current MVP Requirement\n+\n+No user-managed API key, OAuth configuration, or external service account is required for the local MVP demo and test flow.\n+\n+The intended baseline experience is:\n+\n+1. Start the local server.\n+2. Open the app.\n+3. Upload a supported file or use the sample announcement.\n+4. Review the generated analysis.\n+\n+## Manual Actions The User May Still Perform\n+\n+- Choose a sample announcement instead of uploading a document when testing fallback behavior.\n+- Verify final application details against the original announcement before submitting to the real institution.\n+- Open external application or document-issuance links provided by the result screen.\n+\n+## Not Required In This Phase\n+\n+The following are intentionally not required for MVP completion:\n+\n+- Claude or OpenAI API key setup\n+- Google Calendar OAuth\n+- Database provisioning\n+- Cloud deployment credentials\n+\n+## Future Optional Setup\n+\n+These setup steps may be introduced after the dependency-light MVP is validated:\n+\n+- `DOCMATE_LLM_PROVIDER` and provider-specific API key variables for real document analysis\n+- Google OAuth client configuration for direct calendar event creation\n+- Institution-specific link maps for school portals or government issuance sites\n+\n+If these future integrations are added, their setup instructions should stay in this document rather than being mixed into the core product spec.\ndiff --git a/tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json
