immediate next actions such as opening the application page and adding a deadline to a calendar flow.\n+\n+## MVP Scope\n+\n+### Supported Inputs\n+\n+- Text-based PDF documents, primarily 1 to 5 pages.\n+- Plain text uploads or decodable file content for local testing and fallback flows.\n+- Scholarship and youth-policy announcements for university students and young adults.\n+\n+### Supported User Profile Fields\n+\n+- Age\n+- Grade\n+- Region\n+- Occupation\n+- Income decile\n+- Enrollment status\n+\n+### Supported Outputs\n+\n+- Structured summary of the announcement:\n+ - Title\n+ - Application period\n+ - Eligibility conditions\n+ - Benefits\n+ - Required documents\n+ - Application method\n+ - Application URL\n+- Eligibility result:\n+ - `eligible`\n+ - `needs_review`\n+ - `ineligible`\n+- Warning items for risky conditions such as:\n+ - Duplicate application restrictions\n+ - No-edit policies\n+ - Automatic rejection conditions\n+ - Missing-document rejection\n+ - Nationality restrictions\n+- Actionable checklist items with optional related URLs.\n+- Immediate actions for opening the application page and registering the deadline.\n+\n+## Primary User Flow\n+\n+1. The user uploads a document or chooses a built-in sample announcement.\n+2. The user enters profile information.\n+3. The system extracts document text.\n+4. The system analyzes the text into a structured extraction.\n+5. The system evaluates eligibility against the profile.\n+6. The system highlights warnings and generates a checklist.\n+7. The user follows the provided action links.\n+\n+## Functional Requirements\n+\n+### Document Analysis\n+\n+- The system must accept uploaded file content without permanently storing the original file on disk.\n+- The system must extract text from decodable inputs directly.\n+- The system must handle PDFs safely even when a PDF parsing library is unavailable.\n+- The system must preserve a deterministic sample flow so the MVP demo works without external dependencies.\n+\n+### Eligibility Evaluation\n+\n+- The system must use rule-based checks for simple conditions such as age, region, and enrollment.\n+- The system must return `needs_review` when profile data is missing or the condition cannot be interpreted confidently.\n+- The system must avoid optimistic guesses for complex nested conditions.\n+\n+### Warning Detection\n+\n+- The system must detect and surface critical risk language when present in the source text.\n+- The warning list must prioritize issues that can cause submission failure or disqualification.\n+\n+### Checklist Generation\n+\n+- The system must convert required documents and application steps into action-oriented checklist items.\n+- Checklist items should use imperative phrasing so the user can act immediately.\n+\n+### Demo Reliability\n+\n+- The MVP must return a consistent sample analysis result without requiring:\n+ - API keys\n+ - OAuth setup\n+ - database provisioning\n+ - external network access\n+\n+## Non-Goals\n+\n+The MVP explicitly excludes:\n+\n+- Login, subscription, billing, or account management\n+- Persistent database storage and history\n+- Real Google OAuth write access\n+- Automatic document issuance from government or school portals\n+- Image-heavy PDFs and OCR-first pipelines\n+- Legal or contract documents with dense nested exceptions\n+- Full automation for complex eligibility rules\n+\n+## Implementation Constraints\n+\n+- The local MVP must be dependency-light and runnable in restricted environments.\n+- The backend must work with the Python standard library only for the core demo path.\n+- The frontend must render without npm install or external CDN assets.\n+- Real LLM providers and richer frameworks remain extension points, not MVP requirements.\n+\n+## Definition Of Done For The MVP\n+\n+- A user can complete the sample analysis flow end to end on a local machine without network access.\n+- Structured extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and action links are all visible in the product flow.\n+- The implementation remains aligned with the future path toward FastAPI, React, and real LLM providers, but does not depend on them now.\ndiff --git a/docs/user-intervention.md b/docs/user-intervention.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..3c0499d292837c5b05151a792142d8f6311fc57e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/user-intervention.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@\n+# DocMate MVP User Intervention\n+\n+## Current MVP Requirement\n+\n+No user-managed API key, OAuth configuration, or external service account is required for the local MVP demo and test flow.\n+\n+The intended baseline experience is:\n+\n+1. Start the local server.\n+2. Open the app.\n+3. Upload a supported file or use the sample announcement.\n+4. Review the generated analysis.\n+\n+## Manual Actions The User May Still Perform\n+\n+- Choose a sample announcement instead of uploading a document when testing fallback behavior.\n+- Verify final application details against the original announcement before submitting to the real institution.\n+- Open external application or document-issuance links provided by the result screen.\n+\n+## Not Required In This Phase\n+\n+The following are intentionally not required for MVP completion:\n+\n+- Claude or OpenAI API key setup\n+- Google Calendar OAuth\n+- Database provisioning\n+- Cloud deployment credentials\n+\n+## Future Optional Setup\n+\n+These setup steps may be introduced after the dependency-light MVP is validated:\n+\n+- `DOCMATE_LLM_PROVIDER` and provider-specific API key variables for real document analysis\n+- Google OAuth client configuration for direct calendar event creation\n+- Institution-specific link maps for school portals or government issuance sites\n+\n+If 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..2b40d57c7ac7f28d7815f19010d4d2822dd3e50d\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/adr.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@\n+# DocMate MVP ADR\n+\n+## ADR-001: Ship A Dependency-Light MVP First\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Use the Python standard library and a no-build frontend as the baseline implementation path.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The execution environment may block package installation.\n+- The project still needs a full demo and test flow without external setup.\n+- Local reliability matters more than framework fidelity in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The current MVP uses `http.server` instead of FastAPI.\n+- The frontend uses plain JavaScript instead of React.\n+- The architecture must preserve upgrade seams for the originally planned stack.\n+\n+## ADR-002: Keep A Deterministic Sample Provider\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Include built-in sample data and deterministic analysis behavior that work without API keys.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product must remain demoable in restricted environments.\n+- Tests should not depend on network access or model variance.\n+- Early product validation is about workflow usefulness, not model-provider sophistication.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sample outputs must be stable across runs.\n+- Real LLM integrations remain optional extension points.\n+\n+## ADR-003: Prefer Conservative Eligibility Outcomes\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Return `needs_review` whenever conditions are incomplete, ambiguous, or too complex for safe rule evaluation.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- A false \"eligible\" result is more harmful than asking the user to verify a condition.\n+- Scholarship and policy announcements often contain exceptions and edge cases.\n+- The MVP only guarantees high confidence on simple rule patterns.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The product will intentionally avoid over-automation for complex documents.\n+- Missing profile fields are treated as unresolved, not ignored.\n+\n+## ADR-004: Make The First Screen The Working Tool\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+The initial UI must open directly into the analysis workspace rather than a marketing landing page.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product value is demonstrated through action, not explanation.\n+- The user flow starts with upload, profile input, and result inspection.\n+- The MVP should minimize clicks between arrival and analysis.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The frontend prioritizes form and result density over promotional content.\n+- Empty, loading, error, and result states are product-critical.\n+\n+## ADR-005: Exclude Persistence And OAuth From The MVP\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Do not implement database storage, login, or real calendar write access in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- These features add setup and operational complexity without validating the core insight.\n+- The immediate product question is whether action-oriented document analysis is useful.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sessions are ephemeral.\n+- Calendar support is limited to links or generated artifacts rather than direct account integration.\n+- User intervention for keys and OAuth remains documented separately.\ndiff --git a/docs/architecture.md b/docs/architecture.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..d0a65eca9e73611db452598bdff74acafe459395\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/architecture.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,183 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Architecture\n+\n+## Architecture Summary\n+\n+The implemented MVP uses a dependency-light local architecture:\n+\n+- Python backend with standard-library HTTP serving\n+- Static no-build frontend\n+- Deterministic analysis pipeline for demo and tests\n+- Clear extension points for future FastAPI, React, and external LLM providers\n+\n+This is intentionally narrower than the long-term proposal. The goal of the current architecture is local reliability first.\n+\n+## System Components\n+\n+### Frontend\n+\n+- `frontend/index.html`\n+ - Single-screen work surface\n+ - Loads local JavaScript and CSS only\n+- `frontend/src/app.js`\n+ - Handles upload, profile entry, sample loading, analysis requests, and result rendering\n+- `frontend/src/styles.css`\n+ - Presents an operator-style tool UI optimized for scanning and action-taking\n+\n+Responsibilities:\n+\n+- Collect document and profile input\n+- Call backend APIs\n+- Render eligibility, extraction, warnings, checklist, and action links\n+- Fall back to sample data when backend access is unavailable\n+\n+### Backend\n+\n+- `backend/app/models.py`\n+ - Core data structures for profile, extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and analysis response\n+- `backend/app/parser.py`\n+ - Converts uploaded content into text\n+ - Handles safe PDF fallback behavior\n+- `backend/app/analyzer.py`\n+ - Produces structured extraction and warning candidates\n+ - Coordinates sample provider behavior\n+- `backend/app/eligibility.py`\n+ - Applies conservative rule-based eligibility evaluation\n+- `backend/app/checklist.py`\n+ - Converts extracted requirements into actionable checklist items\n+- `backend/app/sample_data.py`\n+ - Supplies deterministic sample announcement text and expected profile/result data\n+- `backend/app/server.py`\n+ - Exposes HTTP endpoints and static file serving\n+- `backend/run.py`\n+ - Starts the local server\n+\n+### Tests\n+\n+- `tests/backend/test_analysis.py`\n+ - Verifies pipeline behavior and rule outcomes\n+- `tests/backend/test_server.py`\n+ - Verifies HTTP routes and payload handling\n+\n+## Runtime Flow\n+\n+1. The user opens the frontend from the same server that exposes the API.\n+2. The frontend requests `GET /api/sample` for the sample workflow or submits `POST /api/analyze`.\n+3. The backend parses the uploaded content into text.\n+4. The analyzer extracts structured fields and warning candidates.\n+5. The eligibility module evaluates the extraction against the profile.\n+6. The checklist module turns extracted requirements into action items.\n+7. The backend returns a single structured analysis response to the frontend.\n+\n+Supporting route:\n+\n+- `GET /health` returns a lightweight status check.\n+\n+## API Shape\n+\n+### `GET /health`\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"status\": \"ok\" }\n+```\n+\n+### `GET /api/sample`\n+\n+Response includes:\n+\n+- Sample source text\n+- Sample profile\n+- Sample analysis result or enough data to build one deterministically\n+\n+### `POST /api/analyze`\n+\n+Request:\n+\n+- Multipart form data or another dependency-light format carrying:\n+ - uploaded file content or raw text\n+ - profile fields\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+ \"extraction\": {},\n+ \"eligibility\": {},\n+ \"warnings\": [],\n+ \"checklist\": [],\n+ \"actions\": []\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Errors must use a simple JSON shape such as:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"detail\": \"...\" }\n+```\n+\n+## Data Model Overview\n+\n+### Profile\n+\n+- `age`\n+- `grade`\n+- `region`\n+- `occupation`\n+- `income_decile`\n+- `enrolled`\n+\n+### DocumentExtraction\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `application_period`\n+- `eligibility_conditions`\n+- `benefits`\n+- `required_documents`\n+- `application_method`\n+- `application_url`\n+\n+### EligibilityResult\n+\n+- `status`\n+- `reasons`\n+- `missing_information`\n+\n+### WarningItem\n+\n+- `severity`\n+- `title`\n+- `evidence`\n+\n+### ChecklistItem\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `description`\n+- `action_url`\n+- `done`\n+\n+### AnalysisResult\n+\n+- `extraction`\n+- `eligibility`\n+- `warnings`\n+- `checklist`\n+- `actions`\n+\n+## Design Constraints\n+\n+- No persistent database in the MVP\n+- No external network requirement for tests or demo\n+- No dependency on FastAPI, React, or third-party PDF parsing libraries for the base flow\n+- No file persistence for user uploads\n+\n+## Extension Path\n+\n+The architecture keeps explicit upgrade points:\n+\n+- Replace the standard-library server with FastAPI when dependency freedom is no longer required.\n+- Replace the no-build frontend with React + TypeScript when a richer component model is needed.\n+- Add provider adapters for Claude or OpenAI after API key setup is available.\n+- Add Google Calendar OAuth once the MVP proves user value.\n+\n+These are planned extensions, not current requirements.\ndiff --git a/docs/spec.md b/docs/spec.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..2b40d57c7ac7f28d7815f19010d4d2822dd3e50d\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/adr.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@\n+# DocMate MVP ADR\n+\n+## ADR-001: Ship A Dependency-Light MVP First\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Use the Python standard library and a no-build frontend as the baseline implementation path.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The execution environment may block package installation.\n+- The project still needs a full demo and test flow without external setup.\n+- Local reliability matters more than framework fidelity in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The current MVP uses `http.server` instead of FastAPI.\n+- The frontend uses plain JavaScript instead of React.\n+- The architecture must preserve upgrade seams for the originally planned stack.\n+\n+## ADR-002: Keep A Deterministic Sample Provider\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Include built-in sample data and deterministic analysis behavior that work without API keys.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product must remain demoable in restricted environments.\n+- Tests should not depend on network access or model variance.\n+- Early product validation is about workflow usefulness, not model-provider sophistication.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sample outputs must be stable across runs.\n+- Real LLM integrations remain optional extension points.\n+\n+## ADR-003: Prefer Conservative Eligibility Outcomes\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Return `needs_review` whenever conditions are incomplete, ambiguous, or too complex for safe rule evaluation.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- A false \"eligible\" result is more harmful than asking the user to verify a condition.\n+- Scholarship and policy announcements often contain exceptions and edge cases.\n+- The MVP only guarantees high confidence on simple rule patterns.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The product will intentionally avoid over-automation for complex documents.\n+- Missing profile fields are treated as unresolved, not ignored.\n+\n+## ADR-004: Make The First Screen The Working Tool\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+The initial UI must open directly into the analysis workspace rather than a marketing landing page.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- The product value is demonstrated through action, not explanation.\n+- The user flow starts with upload, profile input, and result inspection.\n+- The MVP should minimize clicks between arrival and analysis.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- The frontend prioritizes form and result density over promotional content.\n+- Empty, loading, error, and result states are product-critical.\n+\n+## ADR-005: Exclude Persistence And OAuth From The MVP\n+\n+### Decision\n+\n+Do not implement database storage, login, or real calendar write access in the first milestone.\n+\n+### Why\n+\n+- These features add setup and operational complexity without validating the core insight.\n+- The immediate product question is whether action-oriented document analysis is useful.\n+\n+### Consequences\n+\n+- Sessions are ephemeral.\n+- Calendar support is limited to links or generated artifacts rather than direct account integration.\n+- User intervention for keys and OAuth remains documented separately.\ndiff --git a/docs/architecture.md b/docs/architecture.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..d0a65eca9e73611db452598bdff74acafe459395\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/architecture.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,183 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Architecture\n+\n+## Architecture Summary\n+\n+The implemented MVP uses a dependency-light local architecture:\n+\n+- Python backend with standard-library HTTP serving\n+- Static no-build frontend\n+- Deterministic analysis pipeline for demo and tests\n+- Clear extension points for future FastAPI, React, and external LLM providers\n+\n+This is intentionally narrower than the long-term proposal. The goal of the current architecture is local reliability first.\n+\n+## System Components\n+\n+### Frontend\n+\n+- `frontend/index.html`\n+ - Single-screen work surface\n+ - Loads local JavaScript and CSS only\n+- `frontend/src/app.js`\n+ - Handles upload, profile entry, sample loading, analysis requests, and result rendering\n+- `frontend/src/styles.css`\n+ - Presents an operator-style tool UI optimized for scanning and action-taking\n+\n+Responsibilities:\n+\n+- Collect document and profile input\n+- Call backend APIs\n+- Render eligibility, extraction, warnings, checklist, and action links\n+- Fall back to sample data when backend access is unavailable\n+\n+### Backend\n+\n+- `backend/app/models.py`\n+ - Core data structures for profile, extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and analysis response\n+- `backend/app/parser.py`\n+ - Converts uploaded content into text\n+ - Handles safe PDF fallback behavior\n+- `backend/app/analyzer.py`\n+ - Produces structured extraction and warning candidates\n+ - Coordinates sample provider behavior\n+- `backend/app/eligibility.py`\n+ - Applies conservative rule-based eligibility evaluation\n+- `backend/app/checklist.py`\n+ - Converts extracted requirements into actionable checklist items\n+- `backend/app/sample_data.py`\n+ - Supplies deterministic sample announcement text and expected profile/result data\n+- `backend/app/server.py`\n+ - Exposes HTTP endpoints and static file serving\n+- `backend/run.py`\n+ - Starts the local server\n+\n+### Tests\n+\n+- `tests/backend/test_analysis.py`\n+ - Verifies pipeline behavior and rule outcomes\n+- `tests/backend/test_server.py`\n+ - Verifies HTTP routes and payload handling\n+\n+## Runtime Flow\n+\n+1. The user opens the frontend from the same server that exposes the API.\n+2. The frontend requests `GET /api/sample` for the sample workflow or submits `POST /api/analyze`.\n+3. The backend parses the uploaded content into text.\n+4. The analyzer extracts structured fields and warning candidates.\n+5. The eligibility module evaluates the extraction against the profile.\n+6. The checklist module turns extracted requirements into action items.\n+7. The backend returns a single structured analysis response to the frontend.\n+\n+Supporting route:\n+\n+- `GET /health` returns a lightweight status check.\n+\n+## API Shape\n+\n+### `GET /health`\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"status\": \"ok\" }\n+```\n+\n+### `GET /api/sample`\n+\n+Response includes:\n+\n+- Sample source text\n+- Sample profile\n+- Sample analysis result or enough data to build one deterministically\n+\n+### `POST /api/analyze`\n+\n+Request:\n+\n+- Multipart form data or another dependency-light format carrying:\n+ - uploaded file content or raw text\n+ - profile fields\n+\n+Response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+ \"extraction\": {},\n+ \"eligibility\": {},\n+ \"warnings\": [],\n+ \"checklist\": [],\n+ \"actions\": []\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Errors must use a simple JSON shape such as:\n+\n+```json\n+{ \"detail\": \"...\" }\n+```\n+\n+## Data Model Overview\n+\n+### Profile\n+\n+- `age`\n+- `grade`\n+- `region`\n+- `occupation`\n+- `income_decile`\n+- `enrolled`\n+\n+### DocumentExtraction\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `application_period`\n+- `eligibility_conditions`\n+- `benefits`\n+- `required_documents`\n+- `application_method`\n+- `application_url`\n+\n+### EligibilityResult\n+\n+- `status`\n+- `reasons`\n+- `missing_information`\n+\n+### WarningItem\n+\n+- `severity`\n+- `title`\n+- `evidence`\n+\n+### ChecklistItem\n+\n+- `title`\n+- `description`\n+- `action_url`\n+- `done`\n+\n+### AnalysisResult\n+\n+- `extraction`\n+- `eligibility`\n+- `warnings`\n+- `checklist`\n+- `actions`\n+\n+## Design Constraints\n+\n+- No persistent database in the MVP\n+- No external network requirement for tests or demo\n+- No dependency on FastAPI, React, or third-party PDF parsing libraries for the base flow\n+- No file persistence for user uploads\n+\n+## Extension Path\n+\n+The architecture keeps explicit upgrade points:\n+\n+- Replace the standard-library server with FastAPI when dependency freedom is no longer required.\n+- Replace the no-build frontend with React + TypeScript when a richer component model is needed.\n+- Add provider adapters for Claude or OpenAI after API key setup is available.\n+- Add Google Calendar OAuth once the MVP proves user value.\n+\n+These are planned extensions, not current requirements.\ndiff --git a/docs/spec.md b/docs/spec.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..161c4fc8fb709312b1075f0566214e3812d70bab\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/spec.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,122 @@\n+# DocMate MVP Spec\n+\n+## Product Goal\n+\n+DocMate turns scholarship and youth-policy announcement documents into action-ready guidance.\n+The MVP must help a user move from \"I found a document\" to \"I know whether I can apply and what to do next\" without requiring an API key or external service setup.\n+\n+Core outcomes:\n+\n+- Extract the key application details from an uploaded document.\n+- Compare those details against a user profile and return a conservative 3-state eligibility result.\n+- Surface warnings that commonly cause failed applications.\n+- Convert requirements into a concrete checklist with direct action links.\n+- Provide immediate next actions such as opening the application page and adding a deadline to a calendar flow.\n+\n+## MVP Scope\n+\n+### Supported Inputs\n+\n+- Text-based PDF documents, primarily 1 to 5 pages.\n+- Plain text uploads or decodable file content for local testing and fallback flows.\n+- Scholarship and youth-policy announcements for university students and young adults.\n+\n+### Supported User Profile Fields\n+\n+- Age\n+- Grade\n+- Region\n+- Occupation\n+- Income decile\n+- Enrollment status\n+\n+### Supported Outputs\n+\n+- Structured summary of the announcement:\n+ - Title\n+ - Application period\n+ - Eligibility conditions\n+ - Benefits\n+ - Required documents\n+ - Application method\n+ - Application URL\n+- Eligibility result:\n+ - `eligible`\n+ - `needs_review`\n+ - `ineligible`\n+- Warning items for risky conditions such as:\n+ - Duplicate application restrictions\n+ - No-edit policies\n+ - Automatic rejection conditions\n+ - Missing-document rejection\n+ - Nationality restrictions\n+- Actionable checklist items with optional related URLs.\n+- Immediate actions for opening the application page and registering the deadline.\n+\n+## Primary User Flow\n+\n+1. The user uploads a document or chooses a built-in sample announcement.\n+2. The user enters profile information.\n+3. The system extracts document text.\n+4. The system analyzes the text into a structured extraction.\n+5. The system evaluates eligibility against the profile.\n+6. The system highlights warnings and generates a checklist.\n+7. The user follows the provided action links.\n+\n+## Functional Requirements\n+\n+### Document Analysis\n+\n+- The system must accept uploaded file content without permanently storing the original file on disk.\n+- The system must extract text from decodable inputs directly.\n+- The system must handle PDFs safely even when a PDF parsing library is unavailable.\n+- The system must preserve a deterministic sample flow so the MVP demo works without external dependencies.\n+\n+### Eligibility Evaluation\n+\n+- The system must use rule-based checks for simple conditions such as age, region, and enrollment.\n+- The system must return `needs_review` when profile data is missing or the condition cannot be interpreted confidently.\n+- The system must avoid optimistic guesses for complex nested conditions.\n+\n+### Warning Detection\n+\n+- The system must detect and surface critical risk language when present in the source text.\n+- The warning list must prioritize issues that can cause submission failure or disqualification.\n+\n+### Checklist Generation\n+\n+- The system must convert required documents and application steps into action-oriented checklist items.\n+- Checklist items should use imperative phrasing so the user can act immediately.\n+\n+### Demo Reliability\n+\n+- The MVP must return a consistent sample analysis result without requiring:\n+ - API keys\n+ - OAuth setup\n+ - database provisioning\n+ - external network access\n+\n+## Non-Goals\n+\n+The MVP explicitly excludes:\n+\n+- Login, subscription, billing, or account management\n+- Persistent database storage and history\n+- Real Google OAuth write access\n+- Automatic document issuance from government or school portals\n+- Image-heavy PDFs and OCR-first pipelines\n+- Legal or contract documents with dense nested exceptions\n+- Full automation for complex eligibility rules\n+\n+## Implementation Constraints\n+\n+- The local MVP must be dependency-light and runnable in restricted environments.\n+- The backend must work with the Python standard library only for the core demo path.\n+- The frontend must render without npm install or external CDN assets.\n+- Real LLM providers and richer frameworks remain extension points, not MVP requirements.\n+\n+## Definition Of Done For The MVP\n+\n+- A user can complete the sample analysis flow end to end on a local machine without network access.\n+- Structured extraction, eligibility, warnings, checklist, and action links are all visible in the product flow.\n+- The implementation remains aligned with the future path toward FastAPI, React, and real LLM providers, but does not depend on them now.\ndiff --git a/docs/user-intervention.md b/docs/user-intervention.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..3c0499d292837c5b05151a792142d8f6311fc57e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/user-intervention.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@\n+# DocMate MVP User Intervention\n+\n+## Current MVP Requirement\n+\n+No user-managed API key, OAuth configuration, or external service account is required for the local MVP demo and test flow.\n+\n+The intended baseline experience is:\n+\n+1. Start the local server.\n+2. Open the app.\n+3. Upload a supported file or use the sample announcement.\n+4. Review the generated analysis.\n+\n+## Manual Actions The User May Still Perform\n+\n+- Choose a sample announcement instead of uploading a document when testing fallback behavior.\n+- Verify final application details against the original announcement before submitting to the real institution.\n+- Open external application or document-issuance links provided by the result screen.\n+\n+## Not Required In This Phase\n+\n+The following are intentionally not required for MVP completion:\n+\n+- Claude or OpenAI API key setup\n+- Google Calendar OAuth\n+- Database provisioning\n+- Cloud deployment credentials\n+\n+## Future Optional Setup\n+\n+These setup steps may be introduced after the dependency-light MVP is validated:\n+\n+- `DOCMATE_LLM_PROVIDER` and provider-specific API key variables for real document analysis\n+- Google OAuth client configuration for direct calendar event creation\n+- Institution-specific link maps for school portals or government issuance sites\n+\n+If these future integrations are added, their setup instructions should stay in this document rather than being mixed into the core product spec.\ndiff --git a/tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json b/tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json\nindex 96f9126aa406c0256295938198653c6018efda91..8ae19a8b7f9eb53c4bafe09a47c34af12f294151\n--- a/tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json\n+++ b/tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json\n@@ -5,12 +5,12 @@\n \"totalPhases\": 4,\n \"created_at\": \"2026-05-19T09:50:24+0900\",\n \"phases\": [\n- {\n- \"phase\": 0,\n- \"name\": \"docs-update\",\n- \"status\": \"pending\",\n- \"started_at\": \"2026-05-19T09:57:20+0900\"\n- },\n+ {\n+ \"phase\": 0,\n+ \"name\": \"docs-update\",\n+ \"status\": \"completed\",\n+ \"started_at\": \"2026-05-19T09:57:20+0900\"\n+ },\n {\n \"phase\": 1,\n \"name\": \"backend-pipeline\",\n@@ -27,4 +27,4 @@\n \"status\": \"pending\"\n }\n ]\n-}\n\\ No newline at end of file\n+}\n\ntokens used\n97,108\n", "lastMessage": "`phase 0`를 완료했습니다. 기간, 지원 대상, 혜택, 제출 서류, 신청 방법, 신청 URL을 구조화된 결과로 확인할 수 있게 만든다. - 사용자가 나이, 학년, 거주지, 직업, 소득분위 같은 프로필을 입력하면 문서 조건과 비교해 신청 가능, 추가 확인 필요, 신청 불가 중 하나로 자격 결과를 표시한다. - 문서의 제출 서류와 신청 절차를 즉시 실행 가능한 체크리스트로 변환하고 각 항목에 관련 이동 링크를 붙인다. - 수정 불가, 중복 지원 불가, 자동 탈락, 국적 제한 같은 중요 조건과 제한 사항을 경고 카드로 강조한다. - 자동 파싱된 신청 페이지 이동과 마감일 캘린더 등록을 제공하는 MVP 화면을 만든다. ## 구현 디테일 (어떻게 만들 것인가) - 현재 가정은 React + TypeScript 프론트엔드와 FastAPI 백엔드로 구성하는 MVP이다. - PDF 텍스트 추출은 Python 기반 라이브러리(PyMuPDF 우선, 필요 시 pdfplumber)를 사용한다. - AI 분석은 구조화된 JSON 스키마를 기준으로 LLM 호출부를 분리하고, API 키가 없을 때도 개발과 데모가 가능하도록 샘플 분석 모드를 둔다. - 자격 판정은 MVP 범위에서 단순 조건은 rule-based로 비교하고, 복합 조건이나 누락 정보는 보수적으로 "추가 확인 필요"로 처리한다. - 첫 구현은 1~5페이지 텍스트 기반 정책·장학금 PDF와 대학생·청년 대상 공고에 집중한다. ## 제약 조건 (무엇을 안 할 것인가) - 초기 MVP에서는 로그인, 결제, 구독, 기관용 관리자 기능을 구현하지 않는다. - 초기 MVP에서는 이미지 위주 lastMessage:
Phase 1을 완료했습니다. 표준 라이브러리만으로 동작하는 백엔드 파이프라인을 [backend/app/models.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/backend/app/models.py:35), [backend/app/parser.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/backend/app/parser.py:14), [backend/app/analyzer.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/backend/app/analyzer.py:37), [backend/app/server.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/backend/app/server.py:24)에 구현했고, `GET /health`, `GET /api/sample`, `POST /api/analyze`와 정적 파일 서빙까지 포함했습니다. 샘플 공고, 보수적 자격 판정, 경고 추출, 체크리스트/액션 링크 생성도 같이 들어갔습니다. 검증용 `unittest`는 [tests/backend/test_analysis.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/tests/backend/test_analysis.py:11), [tests/backend/test_server.py](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/tests/backend/test_server.py:13)에 추가했습니다.

검증은 `python -m compileall backend`, `python -m unittest discover -s tests/backend -p "test_*.py"`로 수행했고 둘 다 통과했습니다. `tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json`의 phase 1 상태도 [completed로 갱신](C:/Users/DO/Desktop/docmate/tasks/0-docmate-mvp/index.json:16)했고 완료 시각은 `2026-05-19T10:07:53+0900`입니다. 참고로 `tasks/0-docmate-mvp/docs-diff.md`는 현재 작업 폴더가 git 저장소가 아니라 자동 생성되지 않은 상태입니다.
